description:
Implement the 'bd label list' command in the beads-label.el module.

## Command Signature
```
bd label list [issue-id]
```

## Features

- List all labels for a specific issue
- Context-aware: detect current issue from beads-list or beads-show buffer
- Display labels in dedicated *beads-label-list: ISSUE-ID* buffer
- Support interactive command (M-x beads-label-list)
- Show label count in buffer header

## Implementation

1. State variables:
   - beads-label-list--issue-id (from context or prompt)

2. Reader function:
   - beads-label-list-read-issue-id (with completion, context-aware)

3. Command:
   - Interactive command beads-label-list
   - Can be called from transient or directly via M-x
   - Detects context from buffer (beads-show, beads-list)
   - Falls back to completing-read for issue ID

4. Display buffer:
   - Buffer name: *beads-label-list: ISSUE-ID*
   - Format: Simple list of label names, one per line
   - Header shows issue ID and total label count
   - Read-only buffer with special-mode
   - Keybinding 'q' to quit
   - Keybinding 'g' to refresh
   - Keybinding 'a' to add label to this issue
   - Keybinding 'r' to remove label from this issue

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command beads-label-list implemented
- [ ] Context detection from beads-list and beads-show buffers
- [ ] Labels displayed in formatted buffer
- [ ] Buffer shows issue ID and label count in header
- [ ] Keybindings for quit, refresh, add, remove
- [ ] Empty label list handled gracefully
- [ ] ERT tests in test/beads-label-test.el
- [ ] Autoload entry in beads.el
- [ ] Integration tests with :tags '(integration)
title: beads-label.el: Implement bd label list command

title: Active Agents should show agent type name instead of '#1 (backend)' format

description:
## Locations
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code.el:158` - `beads-agent-backend-session-active-p`
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code-ide.el:168`
- `lisp/beads-agent-claudemacs.el:290`

## Problem
After the backend buffer is renamed from `*claude:...` to `*beads-agent[...]`, `find-buffers` will no longer find it because it searches only for the original naming convention.

```elisp
(cl-defmethod beads-agent-backend-session-active-p
    ((_backend beads-agent-backend-claude-code) session)
  (let* ((working-dir (beads-agent-session-working-dir session))
         (buffers (beads-agent-claude-code--find-buffers working-dir)))
    ;; find-buffers searches for "*claude:..." but buffer is now "*beads-agent[...]"!
    (and buffers (buffer-live-p (car buffers)) ...)))
```

## Impact
`session-active-p` will always return nil for active sessions after rename, breaking session tracking and UI updates.

## Fix Options
1. Use mode-based detection (like agent-shell does) instead of name-based
2. Store buffer reference in session object directly
3. Search for both naming patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] session-active-p works after buffer rename
- [ ] Session tracking reliable throughout lifecycle
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix session tracking broken after buffer rename

description:
Update all regex patterns that match issue IDs to support the new hash-based format:
- Old format: bd-123 (PROJECT-NUMBER)
- New format: bd-a1b2, bd-f14c3, bd-3e7a5b (PROJECT-HASH)

Files to update:
- lisp/beads-eldoc.el: beads-eldoc-issue-pattern
- lisp/beads-show.el: beads-show--issue-reference-regex
- Any other pattern matching for issue IDs

New pattern should match: \b([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*-[0-9a-f]+)\b

This is critical as without it, issue references won't be recognized in buffers.
title: Update issue ID patterns to support hash-based IDs

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-agent.el:744-745` - `beads-agent--fetch-issue-async`

## Problem
Buffer reference inside sentinel without defensive check:

```elisp
(let ((exit-code (process-exit-status proc))
      (output (with-current-buffer output-buffer  ;; Could be killed!
                (buffer-string))))
```

If `output-buffer` is killed externally between process completion and sentinel execution, `with-current-buffer` will signal an error.

## Impact
Error when buffer is killed during async operation.

## Fix
Check `buffer-live-p` before accessing:
```elisp
(when (buffer-live-p output-buffer)
  (with-current-buffer output-buffer ...))
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Graceful handling when buffer killed
- [ ] No errors from dead buffer access
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix buffer nil check in async sentinel

description:
Implement automatic transient infix generation from EIEIO slot metadata.

## Implementation

Create functions to auto-generate transient infixes from command class slots:

1. `beads-meta-generate-infix` - Generate single infix from slot
2. `beads-meta-generate-infixes` - Generate all infixes for a command class
3. `beads-meta-define-infixes` - Macro to define infixes at compile time

## Slot Properties Used

- `:transient-key` - Key binding (required for infix generation)
- `:transient-description` - Description text
- `:transient-class` - Class (transient-option, transient-switch, beads-create-transient-multiline)
- `:transient-reader` - Reader function
- `:transient-choices` - Choices list for choice-based options
- `:transient-prompt` - Input prompt
- `:transient-level` - Visibility level (1-7, controls when shown via C-x l)
- `:transient-group` - Group name (e.g., "Required", "Issue attributes", "Content")
- `:transient-section` - Section within group for finer organization
- `:transient-order` - Order within group/section (lower = first)
- `:long-option` - Used for :argument (e.g., "--title=")

## Group/Section Organization

Slots can specify which transient group they belong to:

```elisp
;; Required group (level 1)
(title :transient-group "Required"
       :transient-level 1
       :transient-order 1)

;; Issue attributes group (level 2)
(issue-type :transient-group "Issue attributes"
            :transient-level 2
            :transient-order 1)
(priority :transient-group "Issue attributes"
          :transient-level 2
          :transient-order 2)

;; Content group (level 3)
(description :transient-group "Content"
             :transient-level 3
             :transient-order 1)
```

## Generated Infix Structure

```elisp
;; From slot:
(title :transient-key "t"
       :transient-description "Title (required)"
       :transient-class transient-option
       :transient-reader beads-reader-issue-title
       :transient-group "Required"
       :transient-level 1
       :long-option "--title")

;; Generates equivalent of:
(transient-define-infix beads-create-infix-title ()
  :class 'transient-option
  :key "t"
  :description "Title (required)"
  :argument "--title="
  :reader #'beads-reader-issue-title)
```

## Naming Convention

Generated infixes named: `beads-<command>-infix-<slot-name>`
e.g., `beads-create-infix-title`, `beads-update-infix-status`

## Override Mechanism

Allow manual override by checking if infix already exists before generating.
User-defined infixes take precedence.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `beads-meta-generate-infix` function implemented
- [ ] `beads-meta-generate-infixes` function implemented
- [ ] All transient classes supported (option, switch, multiline)
- [ ] Reader functions properly referenced
- [ ] Choices handled for choice-based options
- [ ] Group/section/level/order properties supported
- [ ] Override mechanism works
- [ ] ERT tests covering generation
- [ ] Lint and compile pass
title: Implement automatic transient infix generation from slot metadata

description:
Refine backend selection behavior:

- If `beads-agent-default-backend` is set and available, use it without prompting
- If no default is set, always prompt user to select from available backends
- This replaces the current auto-select-single behavior which can be confusing

Current behavior: Auto-selects when only one backend is available
New behavior: Only auto-select when user has explicitly configured a default

## New Command: beads-agent-switch-backend

Add `beads-agent-switch-backend` command that:
- Prompts user to select from available backends using rich completion (with annotations showing backend descriptions)
- Uses `beads-agent--backend-completion-table` for consistent completion experience
- Sets `beads-agent-default-backend` to the selected backend
- Optionally persists to custom-file via `customize-save-variable`
- Shows confirmation message with new default

This gives users an easy way to change their preferred backend without manually editing config, with the same rich completion UI used elsewhere in beads-agent.
title: Respect default backend preference, otherwise prompt for selection

description:
All beads buffers should follow a consistent naming convention that includes:

1. **Project prefix**: All beads buffers should include the beads project name/prefix
2. **Worktree**: When operating in a worktree, include the worktree name
3. **Branch**: When applicable, include the current branch name
4. **Issue ID**: Buffers that display a specific issue should include the issue ID
5. **Issue title**: Include the issue title (truncated if needed) for quick identification
6. **Agent type**: Agent buffers should clearly identify the backend (claude-code, claudemacs, etc.)

This makes it easier to:
- Identify which project a buffer belongs to when working with multiple projects
- Distinguish between buffers from main repo vs worktrees
- Quickly find issue-related buffers using buffer switching commands
- Identify buffer contents without switching to them
- Find and switch between agent sessions

Examples of expected buffer names:
- \`*beads-list: myproject*\` - list buffer for myproject
- \`*beads-list: myproject@feature-branch*\` - list buffer in a worktree
- \`*beads-show: myproject/proj-123 Fix login bug*\` - show buffer with title
- \`*beads-show: myproject@worktree/proj-123 Implement auth...*\` - truncated title in worktree
- \`*beads-agent: myproject/claude-code#1*\` - first claude-code agent for project
- \`*beads-agent: myproject@worktree/claudemacs#2 proj-123*\` - agent working on issue in worktree
- \`*beads-agent: myproject/efrit#1 proj-456 Add dark mode*\` - agent with issue ID and title

Current behavior: Buffer names may not consistently include all relevant context, making it hard to identify buffers when switching.
notes: Completed: stats, graph, agent-list buffers migrated. Remaining: sync, labels, import, dep, worktree, quickstart, epic-status, delete, option, show-edit buffers.
title: Standardize beads buffer naming with project prefix, worktree, branch, and issue ID

description:
Build an Emacs Lisp package that provides a Magit-like porcelain interface for Beads issue tracking. The package should live in the lisp/ directory and provide an intuitive, keyboard-driven UI for working with Beads issues directly from Emacs.

Key features:
- Status buffer showing issues by status (open, in_progress, blocked, closed)
- Keyboard shortcuts for common operations (create, update, close, show details)
- Integration with existing beads CLI via subprocess calls
- Transient menus for complex operations (similar to Magit's interface)
- Jump to issue definitions in code
- Inline editing of issue fields
- Dependency visualization
- Integration with Emacs project.el for multi-project support

This will make Beads more accessible to Emacs users and provide a seamless workflow for AI-supervised coding within the editor.
notes:
**Core Goal Update (2025-10-18)**: The primary objective of beads.el is to provide complete 1-to-1 coverage of the Beads CLI. For every 'bd' command and flag, there should be a corresponding Emacs command accessible through the beads.el interface. Users should be able to perform all bd operations without leaving Emacs.

This means:
- Every bd subcommand (init, create, list, show, update, close, ready, blocked, stats, dep, export, import) must have an Emacs equivalent
- All flags and options for each command must be supported in the transient menus
- The Emacs interface should provide the same level of control as the CLI, just with better UX for Emacs users

**New BD CLI Commands Requiring Emacs Implementation (Updated 2025-10-18)**:
The following bd commands exist in the CLI but do not have Emacs implementations yet:

1. **compact** - Compact old closed issues using semantic summarization (tier 1: 30 days, tier 2: 90 days)
2. **daemon** - Run background sync daemon with auto-commit/push capabilities (may be automatic in Emacs context)
3. **delete** - Delete an issue and clean up all references (destructive operation)
4. **epic** - Epic management commands (NEW: subcommands for working with epics)
5. **label** - Manage issue labels (subcommands: add, list, list-all, remove)
6. **rename-prefix** - Rename the issue prefix for all issues in the database
7. **renumber** - Renumber all issues sequentially to eliminate ID gaps
8. **reopen** - Reopen closed issues (more explicit than update --status open)
9. **restore** - Restore full history of a compacted issue from git
10. **sync** - Synchronize issues with git remote in single operation

**Already Implemented Commands**:
The following bd commands already have Emacs implementations:
- blocked, close, create, dep (add/remove/tree/cycles), export, import, init, list, quickstart, ready, show, stats, update

**Commands Not Requiring Emacs Implementation**:
- completion (Emacs has its own completion system)
- help (covered by Emacs help system)
- version (not essential; could be shown in beads main menu if needed)

**Note on Global Flags**:
The bd CLI now has a --no-daemon flag that should be supported in all transient menus alongside existing global flags (--actor, --db, --json, --no-auto-flush, --no-auto-import).
title: beads.el: Magit-like Emacs interface for Beads

description:
Add EIEIO command classes for bd epic subcommands to beads-command.el.

The bd epic command has two subcommands that need command class implementations:

1. **bd epic close-eligible** - Close epics where all children are complete
   - Flags: --dry-run (boolean)
   - Supports: --json (default: t)
   
2. **bd epic status** - Show epic completion status
   - Flags: --eligible-only (boolean)
   - Supports: --json (default: t)

Both commands support all global flags (--actor, --db, --no-auto-flush, --no-auto-import, --no-daemon, --no-db, --sandbox).

## Implementation Details

Create the following command classes in beads-command.el:

### beads-command-epic-close-eligible
- Parent class: beads-command-json
- Slots:
  - dry-run (boolean, initform nil) - Preview what would be closed
  - json (boolean, initform t) - inherited from beads-command-json

### beads-command-epic-status
- Parent class: beads-command-json
- Slots:
  - eligible-only (boolean, initform nil) - Show only epics eligible for closure
  - json (boolean, initform t) - inherited from beads-command-json

## Requirements

1. Implement command classes following existing patterns (like beads-command-list, beads-command-create)
2. Implement beads-command-line methods to build command arguments
3. Implement beads-command-validate methods if needed
4. Implement beads-command-execute methods to parse JSON output
5. Add helper functions like beads-command-epic-close-eligible! and beads-command-epic-status!
title: Add command classes for epic subcommands (close-eligible, status)

description:
The beads-stats command's JSON output format has changed. The bd stats --json output now uses a nested structure with `summary` and `recent_activity` objects instead of a flat structure.

**JSON Structure Change:**
- Old (expected): `{total_issues: 100, open_issues: 30, ...}`
- New (actual): `{summary: {total_issues: 100, ...}, recent_activity: {commit_count: 4, ...}}`

**CLI Format Changes:**
- Header: "📊 Issue Database Status" (not "📊 Beads Statistics:")
- Section headers: "Summary:", "Extended:", "Recent Activity (last 24 hours):"
- Label: "Ready to Work:" (not "Ready:")
- New fields: deferred_issues, tombstone_issues, pinned_issues
- Recent activity: commits, issues created/closed/updated/reopened
- Footer: "For more details, use 'bd list' to see individual issues."
- Right-aligned numbers at fixed column

The Emacs interface must be updated to:
1. Parse the new nested JSON structure
2. Render output matching the CLI format exactly
3. Keep all buttons interactive for filtering issues
notes:
Plan updated. Key changes:
1. Add beads-recent-activity and beads-stats-data types
2. Update JSON parsing for nested structure
3. Rewrite render function to match CLI output format
4. Add 1 blank line padding at top, 1 space padding on left for all content
5. Update all test fixtures to new JSON structure
title: Fix beads-stats command to match bd stats output

description:
The dependency validation regex in beads-reader.el only accepts lowercase letters, but issue IDs support uppercase and other formats including hash-based identifiers.

Current regex: "^[a-z0-9-]+$"
Problem: Rejects valid IDs like "BD-123", "Worker-1", "bd-a3f8e9"

Solution:
1. Review official bd documentation for valid ID formats
2. Update beads-reader-read-dependencies regex to accept:
   - Uppercase letters (BD-, JIRA-, etc.)
   - Hash-based IDs (bd-a3f8e9, etc.)
   - Mixed case project prefixes
3. Add test cases for various valid ID formats
4. Ensure validation matches actual bd CLI behavior

Updated regex should be: "^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]*$" or similar based on actual bd spec.
title: Fix dependency validation regex - too restrictive for uppercase IDs

description:
Integrate show buffers with directory-keyed worktree sessions.

## Purpose
Register show buffers with worktree sessions for proper lifecycle management. Sessions are keyed by directory, not branch.

## Functions to Add
```elisp
(defun beads-show--register-with-session (buffer)
  "Register BUFFER with current worktree session."
  (when-let ((session (beads-sesman--ensure-worktree-session)))
    (cl-pushnew buffer (oref session buffers))
    (sesman-link-session 'Beads session 'buffer buffer)))

(defun beads-show--unregister-from-session ()
  "Remove current buffer from worktree session."
  (when-let ((session (beads-sesman--buffer-session (current-buffer))))
    (setf (oref session buffers)
          (delq (current-buffer) (oref session buffers)))
    (beads-sesman--maybe-cleanup-session session)))
```

## Functions to Modify
- `beads-show` - add registration after buffer creation
- `beads-show-mode` - add kill-buffer-hook

## Acceptance Criteria

### Registration
- [ ] Show buffer registered when created
- [ ] Buffer appears in session's buffers slot
- [ ] Sesman context link created
- [ ] Multiple show buffers in same session

### Unregistration
- [ ] kill-buffer-hook added
- [ ] Buffer removed from session on kill
- [ ] Session cleanup check triggered
- [ ] Hook is buffer-local

### Multiple Show Buffers
- [ ] Session can track multiple show buffers
- [ ] Each show buffer tracked separately
- [ ] Killing one doesn't affect others

### Same Issue, Same Directory
- [ ] Same issue, same project-dir → same buffer (reused)
- [ ] Buffer just refreshed, not duplicated

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Handles no session yet
- [ ] Handles buffer already in session
- [ ] Handles showing same issue twice

### Testing
- [ ] Test: registration adds buffer
- [ ] Test: unregistration removes buffer
- [ ] Test: multiple show buffers in session

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings
- [ ] Passes lint checks
- [ ] All existing show tests pass
title: Integrate show buffers with worktree sessions

description:
Report bug to xenodium/shell-maker (not agent-shell directly):

## Bug Summary
`shell-maker-define-major-mode` fails in batch Emacs with:
  (void-function keymap)

## Root Cause
In shell-maker.el, the function `shell-maker-define-major-mode` has:

```elisp
(eval \`(define-derived-mode ...
         (use-local-map ,mode-map)))  ;; BUG: mode-map is a keymap OBJECT
```

When `mode-map` is a keymap object, `,mode-map` embeds its printed representation `(keymap ...)` into the form. When this is eval'd, Emacs tries to call `keymap` as a function.

## Reproduction
```bash
emacs --batch --eval "(require 'agent-shell)" --eval "(with-temp-buffer (agent-shell-mode))"
```

## Suggested Fix
Quote the keymap value to prevent re-evaluation:

```elisp
(eval \`(define-derived-mode ...
         (use-local-map ',mode-map)))  ;; Quote the value
```

## Impact
- Affects any package using shell-maker with custom keymaps
- Prevents batch-mode testing of agent-shell and derivatives
- beads-agent-agent-shell backend cannot have E2E tests until fixed

## Upstream Repos
- shell-maker: https://github.com/xenodium/shell-maker
- agent-shell: https://github.com/xenodium/agent-shell
title: Report shell-maker batch mode bug upstream

description:
Add a `description` slot to the `beads-agent-backend` class and display it as
the last element in backend completion annotations.

## Why

Currently backend completions show `[P10] Available` but don't explain what
each backend does. Users unfamiliar with the backends have no context for
choosing between them.

## Implementation

1. Add `description` slot to `beads-agent-backend` class (beads-agent-backend.el)
2. Update `beads-completion--backend-annotate` to append description
3. Update each backend registration to include description

## Suggested Descriptions

Research on existing backends:

| Backend | Suggested Description |
|---------|----------------------|
| claudemacs | AI pair programming via eat terminal (cpoile/claudemacs) |
| claude-code-ide | MCP-based Claude Code integration via claude-code-ide.el |
| claude-code | Lightweight Claude Code via vterm/eat (stevemolitor) |
| agent-shell | AI agent via shell integration (xenodium/agent-shell) |
| efrit | Emacs-native AI agent framework with async execution |
| mock | Testing backend (not for production use) |

## Expected Result

```
Backend: claudemacs    [P10] Available - AI pair programming via eat terminal
         agent-shell   [P20] Available - AI agent via shell integration
         efrit         [P30] Unavailable - Emacs-native AI agent framework
```

## Files to Modify

1. lisp/beads-agent-backend.el - Add description slot to class
2. lisp/beads-completion.el - Update annotation function
3. lisp/beads-agent-*.el - Add descriptions to backend registrations
4. test/beads-completion-test.el - Update tests
title: Add description slot to backend class and show in completions

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-agent.el:143-166` - `beads-agent--extract-json`

## Problem
Tries to find JSON in output with string matching but doesn't validate the extracted substring is valid JSON. If output contains "[" or "{" in non-JSON context, could extract garbage.

Example: if bd outputs "Warning: {skipped}", might extract "{skipped}" which isn't valid JSON.

## Fix
Validate JSON before returning or handle parse errors gracefully.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clear error for malformed output
- [ ] Test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Validate extracted JSON before returning

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd compact' command.

The bd compact command uses semantic summarization to reduce database size by compacting old closed issues. This is permanent graceful decay where original content is discarded.

**Features:**
- Two compression tiers:
  - Tier 1: Semantic compression (30 days closed, 70% reduction)
  - Tier 2: Ultra compression (90 days closed, 95% reduction)
- Parallel processing with configurable workers
- Batch processing with configurable batch size
- Preview mode with --dry-run
- Statistics display
- Force compact specific issues

**Implementation:**
- Create beads-compact.el module
- Transient menu with all flags:
  - --all: Process all candidates
  - --dry-run: Preview without compacting
  - --stats: Show compaction statistics
  - --id: Compact specific issue
  - --force: Force compact (bypass checks, requires --id)
  - --tier: Compaction tier (1 or 2)
  - --batch-size: Issues per batch
  - --workers: Parallel workers
- Display results in dedicated buffer
- Show before/after statistics
- Confirmation prompt for destructive operations
title: beads.el: Implement bd compact command

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd daemon' command.

The bd daemon command runs a background sync daemon that automatically synchronizes issues with git remote at configurable intervals.

**Features:**
- Start daemon with auto-commit/push capabilities
- Stop running daemon
- Check daemon status
- Configurable sync interval
- Custom log file path
- Automatic JSONL export/import
- Pull remote changes periodically

**Implementation:**
- Create beads-daemon.el module or add to beads-misc.el
- Transient menu with all flags:
  - --auto-commit: Automatically commit changes
  - --auto-push: Automatically push commits
  - --interval: Sync check interval (duration)
  - --log: Log file path
  - --status: Show daemon status
  - --stop: Stop running daemon
- Display daemon status in modeline or dedicated buffer
- Show log output in buffer
- Start/stop controls
title: beads.el: Implement bd daemon command

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd delete' command.

The bd delete command permanently deletes an issue and cleans up all references to it. This is a destructive operation that:
1. Removes all dependency links (any type, both directions)
2. Updates text references to '[deleted:ID]' in directly connected issues
3. Deletes the issue from the database

**Implementation:**
- Add to beads-misc.el or create beads-delete.el
- Transient menu with flags:
  - -f/--force: Actually delete (without this, shows preview)
- Preview mode shows:
  - Issue details
  - All dependencies that will be removed
  - All text references that will be updated
- Confirmation prompt with detailed warning
- Display results after deletion
- Context-aware: can delete current issue from show/list buffer
title: beads.el: Implement bd delete command

description: Research the with-simulated-input package to determine if it can be used for testing transient menus in batch mode. The package claims to provide a macro that evaluates forms while simulating input events, reimplemented using execute-kbd-macro to work in batch mode.
notes:
Research findings:

**with-simulated-input Package**:
- Available on MELPA/NonGNU ELPA
- Designed for non-interactive testing of interactive functions
- Works in batch mode by using execute-kbd-macro internally
- Can mix key sequences and Lisp code: (with-simulated-input ("key" (do-something) "RET"))

**Critical Issue for Transient Testing** (magit/transient#180):
- Transients fail in batch mode when called with funcall
- Solution: Must use call-interactively to trigger pre/post-command-hooks
- Hooks are essential for transient state management

**Recommendation**:
NOT viable for our integration testing needs because:
1. Transients require interactive event loop
2. Our current approach (mocking transient-args + call-interactively) is simpler and more reliable
3. with-simulated-input adds complexity without clear benefits for suffix command testing

**Alternative**: Continue with current integration test pattern using beads-test-with-transient-args macro
title: Research and evaluate with-simulated-input package for transient testing

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd label' command and all subcommands.

The bd label command manages issue labels with four subcommands:
1. **label add** - Add a label to an issue
2. **label remove** - Remove a label from an issue
3. **label list** - List labels for a specific issue
4. **label list-all** - List all unique labels in the database

**Implementation:**
- Create beads-label.el module
- Parent transient menu beads-label with subcommands
- beads-label-add: prompt for issue ID and label name
- beads-label-remove: prompt for issue ID and label (with completion)
- beads-label-list: show labels for issue in buffer
- beads-label-list-all: show all labels in tabulated-list buffer
- Display labels in beads-list and beads-show buffers
- Support multiple labels per issue
- Label completion from existing labels
title: beads.el: Implement bd label command and subcommands

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd rename-prefix' command.

The bd rename-prefix command renames the issue prefix for all issues in the database, updating:
- All issue IDs
- All text references across all fields
- All dependency links

**Prefix validation rules:**
- Max length: 8 characters
- Allowed: lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens
- Must start with a letter
- Must end with a hyphen (e.g., 'kw-', 'work-')

**Implementation:**
- Add to beads-misc.el
- Transient menu with:
  - New prefix input with validation
  - --dry-run flag
- Preview mode shows old->new ID mappings
- Strong confirmation prompt
- Display results with mapping table
- This is a destructive operation
title: beads.el: Implement bd rename-prefix command

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd renumber' command.

The bd renumber command renumbers all issues sequentially to eliminate gaps in the ID space, updating:
- All issue IDs (starting from 1, keeping chronological order)
- All dependency links
- All text references in all fields

**Risks:**
- May break external references (GitHub issues, docs, commits)
- Git history may become confusing

description:
Research how agent backends handle buffer names. We want all backends spawned in a buffer controlled by beads. The buffer should be named after the agent type name, the issue id and the numbered instance of the agent.

Example buffer name format: *beads-agent[beads.el-xrrt][Plan#1]*

Components:
- beads-agent prefix
- issue ID in brackets
- agent type name with instance number

This requires understanding:
1. How each backend currently creates/names buffers
2. How to pass buffer naming control to beads
3. How to track instance numbers per issue/type combination

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Excellent test coverage for buffer naming functionality
- [ ] New Emacs server controller test harness using emacsclient
- [ ] Test harness exercises and verifies buffer naming works for all agent backends:
  - [ ] claude-code-ide
  - [ ] claude-code
  - [ ] claudemacs
  - [ ] agent-shell
  - [ ] efrit
  - [ ] mock
notes:
## Implementation Complete

### Buffer Naming Infrastructure (beads-agent-backend.el)

Added comprehensive buffer naming support:

1. **Core Functions**:
   - `beads-agent--generate-buffer-name` - Creates names in format `*beads-agent[ISSUE-ID][TYPE#N]*`

   - `beads-agent--buffer-name-p` - Predicate to check valid buffer names

2. **Per-(issue, type) Instance Tracking**:

   - `beads-agent--peek-typed-instance-number` - Peek without incrementing
   - `beads-agent--reset-typed-instance-counters` - Reset for testing

3. **Buffer Discovery**:
   - `beads-agent--find-buffers-by-issue` - Find all buffers for an issue
   - `beads-agent--find-buffers-by-type` - Find all buffers by type name

### Test Coverage (beads-agent-test.el)

## Location
`lisp/beads-list.el:1130-1137`

## Problem
Declares hook variable that shadows definition in beads-agent-backend.el:
```elisp
(defvar beads-agent-state-change-hook)  ;; Shadows real definition!
```

Circular dependency risk between beads-list, beads-sesman, and beads-agent-backend.

## Fix
Use `defvar` with proper require ordering, or use `declare-function` pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No variable shadowing
- [ ] Clean module dependencies

title: Fix hook registration order dependency

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-agent.el:762` - `beads-agent--fetch-issue-async`

## Problem
When JSON parsing fails in `beads-agent--fetch-issue-async`, the callback is NEVER invoked. This orphans the async operation and prevents completion of the agent start flow.

```elisp
(error
 (message "Failed to parse issue: %s" (error-message-string err)))
;; MISSING: (funcall callback ...)
```

## Impact
When `beads--parse-issue` throws an error, the callback is never called. This leaves `beads-agent--start-async` waiting indefinitely for the issue to be processed, blocking subsequent operations.

## Fix
Add `(funcall callback (beads-issue ...))` with a fallback issue object, or propagate the error to allow graceful failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Callback is always invoked, even on parse error
- [ ] Error is properly logged/reported to user
- [ ] Excellent test coverage for error path
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix missing callback on JSON parse error

description:
When running M-x beads-create and then trying to reset previously set flags or title using the Reset command (R key), it raises a wrong-number-of-arguments error instead of resetting the values.

Error: (wrong-number-of-arguments (1 . 1) 2)

The issue is in beads-create--reset at line 198 in beads-create.el:
(transient-set-value (transient-prefix-object) nil)

This calls transient-set-value with 2 arguments, but it only accepts 1 argument. The correct approach is to use transient-reset which is the proper API for resetting transient state.
title: beads-create reset command raises wrong-number-of-arguments error

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd restore' command.

The bd restore command restores full history of a compacted issue from git version control. When an issue is compacted, the git commit hash is saved. This command:
1. Reads the compacted_at_commit from the database
2. Checks out that commit temporarily
3. Reads the full issue from JSONL at that point in history
4. Displays the full issue history
5. Returns to the current git state

This is read-only and does not modify database or git state.

**Implementation:**
- Add to beads-compact.el or beads-misc.el
- Simple command: beads-restore ISSUE-ID
- Display restored issue in special buffer
- Show full description, events, history
- Indicate it's a historical view
- Context-aware: can restore current compacted issue

description:
Create directory-aware buffers AND agents for beads.el that work with project/worktree directories as identity, with git branch and current issue as optional metadata.

## Key Insight
**Directory is identity. Branch and issue are metadata.**

This applies to BOTH buffers AND agent sessions:
- Directory is stable (doesn't change on branch switch)
- Directory is unique (no collisions)
- Directory determines where .beads/ lives
- Directory is where commands run (default-directory)

## Critical Behavioral Changes

### Buffers
**Branch switch in same directory → SAME buffer** (not a new buffer!)

The old branch-aware design was wrong: switching branches in the same repo would create orphaned buffers.

### Agents
**Issue close/switch → SAME agent session** (not a new session!)

Agent sessions persist across issues. An agent lives in a project directory and can work on multiple issues within one session.

## Key Deliverables

### Buffers (existing scope)
1. Buffer identity based on project root directory
2. Separate buffers per worktree/project location
3. Git branch as metadata for display (mode-line, not buffer name)
4. Context preservation via buffer-local variables

### Agents (new scope)
5. Agent session identity based on project directory (not issue)
6. Session ID: `project-name#N` (e.g., `beads.el#1`)
7. Buffer name: `*beads-agent[project-name][#N]*`
8. `current-issue`: focus issue (can change mid-session)
9. `touched-issues`: all issues worked on (for tracking)
10. Session persistence when closing/switching issues

## Buffer Naming
Simple names when one project, disambiguated when multiple:
- List: `*beads-list*` or `*beads-list<beads.el>*`
- Show: `*beads-show: bd-42*` or `*beads-show: bd-42<beads.el>*`
- Agent: `*beads-agent[beads.el][#1]*`

## Agent Session Model

| Aspect | Old (Issue-Bound) | New (Directory-Bound) |
|--------|-------------------|----------------------|
| Identity | issue-id | project-dir |
| Session ID | `bde-42#1` | `beads.el#1` |
| Buffer | `*beads-agent[bd-42][Task#1]*` | `*beads-agent[beads.el][#1]*` |
| Multi-issue | New session each | Same session, switch focus |
| Continuity | Lost on issue close | Preserved |

## Session Identity
Sessions keyed by project root directory (not issue or branch):
- `~/code/beads.el` or just `beads.el` if unique

See plans:
- ~/.claude/plans/foamy-painting-gizmo.md (buffers)
- ~/.claude/plans/bubbly-petting-garden.md (agents)
notes:
**Clarification from testing (2025-12-22):**

Agent buffer naming should be: `*beads-agent[PROJECT-NAME][TYPE#N]*`

The agent type (Plan, Task, Review, etc.) is valuable information and should be kept.
Example: `*beads-agent[beads.el][Plan#1]*`

See beads.el-dtkq for the fix.
title: Directory-aware buffer system with sesman worktree sessions

description:
The PR #19 code review identified that the test suite moved from unit tests to integration tests, which has trade-offs. We should add back unit tests that don't require the bd CLI.

## Current State

The test suite currently uses integration tests that execute actual bd commands in temporary projects. While this tests real behavior, it:
- Is slower than unit tests
- Requires bd CLI to be available
- Doesn't test individual components in isolation
- Makes it harder to test edge cases and error conditions

## What to Add

Add unit tests for command class internals that don't require bd execution:

### 1. Command Construction Tests
Test that command classes build correct argument lists:
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-command-test-list-command-line ()
  "Test beads-command-list builds correct arguments."
  (let ((cmd (beads-command-list 
              :status "open" 
              :priority 1
              :no-daemon t)))
    (should (member "--status" (beads-command-line cmd)))
    (should (member "open" (beads-command-line cmd)))
    (should (member "--priority" (beads-command-line cmd)))
    (should (member "--no-daemon" (beads-command-line cmd)))))
```

### 2. Validation Tests
Test validation logic without executing commands:
- Priority range validation (0-4)
- Type validation (bug, feature, task, etc.)
- Required field validation (title, issue-id, etc.)
- Dependency format validation
- Option conflict detection

### 3. Argument Building Edge Cases
- Empty/nil values
- Special characters in strings
- Unicode handling
- Very long strings
- Multiple values (labels, dependencies)

### 4. Class Instantiation
- Default values
- Slot type checking
- Inheritance chain

## Benefits

- Faster test execution
- Better test isolation
- Easier to test edge cases
- Can run without bd CLI installed
- Complements integration tests

## Implementation

- Keep existing integration tests (they verify actual bd behavior)
- Add new unit test files or sections in existing test files
- Aim for >80% code coverage on command class methods
- Focus on beads-command-line, beads-command-validate, argument building

## Related

This addresses feedback from PR #19 code review comment about test coverage concerns.
title: Add unit tests for command classes alongside integration tests

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-completion.el:76-96` - `beads-completion--issue-annotate`

## Problem
Silently returns empty string on ANY error:
```elisp
(condition-case nil
    ...
  (error ""))  ;; Silent failure!
```

Makes debugging completion issues very difficult.

## Fix
Log errors in debug mode or when a debug variable is set.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Errors logged when debugging
- [ ] Silent in normal use
- [ ] Test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Log errors in completion annotation function

description:
Update the beads.el installation documentation to show how to install
from source using use-package, since the package is not yet on MELPA.

The current documentation (likely in README.md) needs to be updated to
provide clear installation instructions for users who want to install
beads.el directly from the git repository.

**Required changes:**
- Update README.md installation section
- Remove or clarify any references to MELPA installation
- Add use-package configuration example with :load-path or :vc
- Include any required dependencies (transient, etc.)
- Show how to set up autoloads properly
- Add quickstart section for first-time setup

**Example use-package configuration to include:**
```elisp
;; Option 1: Using :load-path (after git clone)
(use-package beads
  :load-path "~/path/to/beads.el/lisp"
  :commands (beads beads-list beads-show beads-create)
  :bind (("C-c b" . beads)))

;; Option 2: Using :vc (Emacs 29+)
(use-package beads
  :vc (:fetcher github :repo "user/beads.el")
  :commands (beads beads-list beads-show beads-create))
```

**Also document:**
- Manual installation steps (git clone, add to load-path)
- Required Emacs version (27.1+)
- Required dependencies
- How to byte-compile for better performance

description:
Integrate Eldev (Emacs Lisp Development) tool into the beads.el
project to streamline development, testing, and linting workflows.

Eldev provides:
- Dependency management (install transient, etc.)
- Test running with multiple Emacs versions
- Linting (checkdoc, package-lint, elisp-lint)
- Byte compilation with warnings
- CI/CD integration
- Consistent development environment

**Current state:**
- Tests run via Makefile (make test, make test-*)
- No automated linting
- No multi-version Emacs testing
- Manual dependency management

**Proposed changes:**
- Add Eldev configuration file
- Configure test runner to use existing ERT tests
- Add linting targets (checkdoc, package-lint, elisp-lint)
- Set up CI workflow using Eldev (GitHub Actions)
- Document Eldev usage in README.md
- Keep Makefile for compatibility (can call Eldev commands)

**Eldev configuration should include:**
- Minimum Emacs version (27.1)
- Dependencies (transient)
- Test files location (lisp/test/)

- Build targets

**Benefits:**
- Consistent dev environment across machines
- Automated code quality checks
- Multi-version testing (Emacs 27.1, 28.x, 29.x)

description:
Complete the file structure reorganization by consolidating core beads.el test 
files and renaming the test helper file.

## Changes needed

### Merge core test files into beads-test.el
Currently we have test files without corresponding implementation files:
- beads-utils-test.el (tests logging, error handling, customization)
- beads-process-test.el (tests process execution, JSON parsing)
- beads-project-test.el (tests project.el integration, .beads discovery)
- beads-tramp-test.el (tests Tramp remote support)

All of these test functionality from the core beads.el module. Merge them into
a single beads-test.el file to achieve 1-to-1 correspondence between 
implementation and test files.

### Rename test helper file
- beads-testing.el → beads-test-helper.el

This makes it clear it's not a test file itself, but provides shared utilities
for the test suite.

## Expected outcome

After this change, every *-test.el file will have exactly one corresponding
implementation file:
- beads-test.el → beads.el
- beads-close-test.el → beads-close.el
- beads-create-test.el → beads-create.el
- etc.

And beads-test-helper.el will clearly indicate it's a helper/utility file.

All tests must still pass after the merge.
title: Consolidate core beads.el tests and rename test helper file

description:
## Overview

Currently only beads-delete has integration tests (marked with :tags 
'(integration)). We need comprehensive integration tests for all 
commands that test functionality at the user interaction level.

## Reference Implementation

See lisp/test/beads-delete-test.el lines 260-270 for the integration 
test pattern. Integration tests should:

1. Be marked with :tags '(integration)
2. Test real user workflows (keybindings, buffer modes, context 
   detection)
3. Test end-to-end command execution from UI entry points
4. Avoid excessive mocking - test actual integration between modules

## Commands Needing Integration Tests

### High Priority
- beads-create: Test transient menu workflow, field validation, 
  issue creation from different contexts
- beads-update: Test context-aware updates from list/show modes, 
  transient menu
- beads-close: Test closing from different contexts, reason 
  requirement
- beads-list: Test keybindings (RET, TAB, g, D, etc.), navigation, 
  filtering
- beads-show: Test rendering, navigation (C-c C-n/p/f/b/u), 
  clickable references
  
### Medium Priority  
- beads-dep: Test dependency management commands, graph navigation
- beads-graph: Test graph generation and visualization
- beads-stats: Test statistics display and formatting
- beads-misc: Test import/export, archive commands

### Cross-Module Integration
- beads-main: Test root transient menu navigation to subcommands
- Context switching: Test moving between list → show → edit workflows
- Cache invalidation: Test that mutations properly refresh UI buffers

## Success Criteria

- Each command module has integration tests in its test file
- Tests verify keybindings work in appropriate major modes
- Tests verify commands work from different contexts (list mode, 
  show mode, no context)
- Tests verify buffer creation, navigation, and cleanup
- All integration tests pass with: eldev test -t integration
- Remove unit tests that duplicate integration test coverage

## Implementation Notes

- Use beads-test-helper for common mocking patterns
- Keep process-file mocking but test actual function integration
- Test actual buffer creation, keybindings, and mode behavior
- Focus on "does this work like a user would expect?"
title: Add integration tests for all commands

description:
Review and standardize key bindings throughout beads.el for consistency
and alignment with popular Emacs modes (Magit, dired, ibuffer, etc.).

## Current Issues

### Inconsistent Buffer-Level Keys
- Close: `d` or `k` in list mode
- Reopen: `o` in list mode
- Create: `c` or `+` in list mode
- Update: `e` in list mode
- Delete: `D` in list mode

Some of these conflict with common Emacs conventions or are inconsistent
with each other.

### Inconsistent Transient Menu Execute Keys
Different transient menus use different keys for the main action:
- beads-create: `x` to execute
- beads-update: `x` to execute
- beads-close: `x` to execute
- beads-reopen: `x` to execute
- Other menus may vary

We should standardize on one key (probably `x` or `RET`) across all
transient menus.

## Research Conventions

Study key bindings from popular modes:
- **Magit**: How does it handle similar operations?
- **dired**: File operations (delete, rename, etc.)
- **ibuffer**: Buffer operations (mark, delete, etc.)
- **org-mode**: Task state changes
- **package-menu-mode**: Install/delete operations

## Proposed Changes

### Buffer-Level Keys
Consider these conventions:
- `RET` - Show/view issue details (current)
- `c` - Create new issue (current, matches dired `C` for copy/create)
- `e` - Edit/update issue (current, matches dired edit)
- `d` - Mark for deletion/closing (like dired `d` for delete)
- `x` - Execute marked operations (like dired `x`)
- `k` - Kill/close immediately (like `k` in many modes)
- `o` - Reopen (current)
- `D` - Delete permanently (current, matches dired `D`)

Or alternative:
- `c` - Close issue (more intuitive than `d`?)
- `r` - Reopen issue (instead of `o`?)

### Transient Menu Keys
Standardize execute key across all transient menus:
- Primary action: `x` (execute) or `RET` or `C-c C-c`
- Preview: `P` or `C-c C-p`
- Reset: `R` or `C-c C-k`
- Quit: `q` or `C-c C-q`

### Bulk Operations
- `B` prefix for bulk operations (current)
- Consistent with buffer-level operations where possible

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document current key bindings across all modes
- [ ] Research conventions from popular Emacs modes
- [ ] Propose consistent key binding scheme
- [ ] Get user feedback on proposal
- [ ] Update all key bindings consistently
- [ ] Update documentation (README, docstrings)
- [ ] Update tests for new key bindings
- [ ] Verify no conflicts with standard Emacs keys
- [ ] All existing functionality still accessible

## Notes

This is a breaking change for existing users, so:
- Consider adding a compatibility layer or migration guide
- Document changes clearly in CHANGELOG
- Consider making key bindings customizable
- Test thoroughly to ensure no regressions
notes:
Additional insights from discussion:

## RET vs x for Execute

RET (Return/Enter) might be better than `x` for executing commands because:
- More intuitive - "press Enter to do it"
- Standard in many Emacs interfaces (completing-read, etc.)
- One less keystroke (no Shift needed)
- Muscle memory from other applications

If RET works, consider using it as primary execute key with `x` as alternative.

## Main Menu vs Bulk Operations Consistency

KEY INSIGHT: Bindings should be consistent between:
1. Main transient menu (M-x beads)
2. Buffer-level single operations (e.g., `c` in list mode)
3. Bulk operations (e.g., `B c` in list mode)

Example inconsistency to fix:
- If main menu uses `c` for create
- And list mode uses `c` for create
- And bulk prefix is `B`
- Then bulk operations should mirror: `B c`, `B e`, `B d`, etc.

Current state to check:
- Main menu: What keys are used?
- List mode single: `c` create, `e` update, `d`/`k` close, `o` reopen, `D` delete
- List mode bulk: `B s` (status), `B p` (priority), `B c` (close), `B o` (reopen)

Notice: Bulk uses different keys than single operations!
- Single: `e` for update
- Bulk: `B s` for status, `B p` for priority (no `B e` for update?)

Proposal: Make bulk operations use same keys as single operations
- `B c` - bulk close (matches `d`/`k` single close)
- `B o` - bulk reopen (matches `o` single reopen)  
- `B e` - bulk update (matches `e` single update)
- `B d` - bulk delete? (matches `D` single delete)

This makes it easier to remember: "press B before the normal key to do bulk operation"
title: Revisit and standardize key bindings across beads.el

description:

description:

- Timer firing after session/buffer destroyed

description:
Update beads-show.el to use project directory as buffer identity.

## Key Change
**Directory is identity, branch is metadata.**

Show buffers are identified by (issue-id, project-dir) pair, not by buffer name with branch.

## Buffer-Local Variables
```elisp
(defvar-local beads-show--project-dir nil
  "Project root directory. THIS IS PART OF THE IDENTITY.")

(defvar-local beads-show--branch nil
  "Git branch name. METADATA for display, not identity.")
```

## Functions to Add

### Buffer Lookup
```elisp
(defun beads-show--find-buffer-for-issue (issue-id project-dir)
  "Find existing show buffer for ISSUE-ID in PROJECT-DIR."
  (cl-find-if
   (lambda (buf)
     (with-current-buffer buf
       (and (derived-mode-p 'beads-show-mode)
            (equal beads-show--issue-id issue-id)
            (equal beads-show--project-dir project-dir))))
   (buffer-list)))
```

### Buffer Get-or-Create
```elisp
(defun beads-show--get-or-create-buffer (issue-id)
  "Get or create show buffer for ISSUE-ID in current project."
  (let* ((project-dir (or (beads--find-project-root) default-directory))
         (existing (beads-show--find-buffer-for-issue issue-id project-dir)))
    (or existing
        (let ((buffer (get-buffer-create (format "*beads-show: %s*" issue-id))))
          (with-current-buffer buffer
            (setq beads-show--project-dir project-dir)
            (setq beads-show--branch (beads--get-git-branch)))
          buffer))))
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### Buffer Identity
- [ ] Lookup by (issue-id, project-dir) pair
- [ ] Same issue, same project-dir → reuses buffer
- [ ] Same issue, different project-dir → different buffers (worktrees)
- [ ] **Branch switch → same buffer** (critical\!)

### Buffer Naming
- [ ] `*beads-show: bd-42*` format (simple)
- [ ] Branch NOT in buffer name
- [ ] Issue ID clearly visible

### Buffer-Local State
- [ ] `beads-show--project-dir` set on creation
- [ ] `beads-show--branch` set as metadata
- [ ] `beads-show--issue-id` preserved (existing)
- [ ] Context survives refresh

### Context Preservation
- [ ] Refresh uses stored project-dir
- [ ] Update/close commands use correct context
- [ ] Clicking issue links uses buffer's project context

### Testing
- [ ] Test: same issue, same dir → same buffer
- [ ] Test: same issue, different dir → different buffers
- [ ] Test: branch switch → same buffer
- [ ] Test: context preserved on refresh

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings
- [ ] Passes lint checks
- [ ] All existing show tests pass
title: Update show buffer for directory-aware identity

description:
Refactor beads-create.el to use the modern transient-args pattern instead of maintaining duplicate state variables.

**Current Pattern (Duplicate State):**
- Maintains state variables: beads-create--title, beads-create--type, etc.
- Custom :reader functions manually sync to state variables
- Execute function reads from state variables
- Requires manual state management and reset logic

**New Pattern (Single Source of Truth):**
- Remove state variables
- Use standard transient infixes without custom readers
- Execute function calls (transient-args 'beads-create)
- Parse arguments into values
- Reset calls (transient-set) to clear transient's state

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth (transient manages state)
- ~50-100 lines less code
- More maintainable
- Consistent with beads-sync.el
- Follows standard transient usage patterns

**Reference:**
See beads-sync.el for the new pattern implementation.
title: Refactor beads-create to use transient-args pattern

description:
Refactor beads-update.el to use the modern transient-args pattern instead of maintaining duplicate state variables.

**Current Pattern:**
- State variables: beads-update--status, beads-update--priority, beads-update--type, etc.

- Complex change tracking logic
- Manual state reset

**New Pattern:**
- Remove state variables
- Use (transient-args 'beads-update)
- Parse arguments to extract values
- Simpler change detection
- (transient-set) for reset

**Benefits:**
- Consistent with beads-sync.el

- Standard transient patterns
- Easier to maintain

**Reference:**
See beads-sync.el and bde-52 for pattern.

description:
## Locations
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code.el:142-151`
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code-ide.el:156-165`

## Problem
`beads-agent-backend-stop` kills the buffer but doesn't explicitly stop the underlying process:

```elisp
(cl-defmethod beads-agent-backend-stop
    ((backend beads-agent-backend-claude-code) session)
  (when-let ((buffer (beads-agent-backend-get-buffer backend session)))
    (when (buffer-live-p buffer)
      (kill-buffer buffer))))  ;; Only kills buffer, not process!
```

Killing the buffer may not terminate the associated `claude` CLI process if the terminal emulator (vterm/eat) has detached the process.

## Impact
Zombie processes left running after session stop. Resource leak.

## Comparison
`beads-agent-claudemacs.el:278-281` does it correctly - explicitly calls `(eat-kill-process)` before killing the buffer.

## Fix
Explicitly kill process before buffer:
```elisp
(when-let ((proc (get-buffer-process buffer)))
  (delete-process proc))
(kill-buffer buffer)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No zombie processes after session stop
- [ ] Process explicitly terminated before buffer
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix zombie processes on session stop

description:
Standardize all transient menu implementations to use the modern transient-args pattern for consistency and maintainability.

**Current State:**
- beads-sync uses the new pattern (reading from transient-args)
- All other modules use the old pattern (duplicate state variables)
- Inconsistent codebase with two different approaches

**Goal:**
Migrate all transient menus to the beads-sync pattern:
1. Remove duplicate state variables
2. Use standard transient infixes
3. Read from (transient-args) in execute functions
4. Use (transient-set) for reset

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth (transient manages state)
- ~300-400 lines less code across the project
- Consistent patterns across all modules
- Easier to maintain and extend

description:
Add an Emacs version of 'bd epic status' that provides an interactive buffer for viewing and navigating epics with their sub-issues.

## Current State

The bd CLI command 'bd epic status' shows all epics with progress:
```
○ beads.el-7bea Update beads.el for beads 0.21.5 compatibility
   Progress: 3/17 children closed (17%)

○ bde-1 beads.el: Magit-like Emacs interface for Beads
   Progress: 3/11 children closed (27%)
```

## Desired State

An interactive Emacs buffer with:
1. **TAB to expand/collapse**: Toggle display of sub-issues inline
2. **RET to navigate**: Open sub-issues in beads-list view
3. **Dynamic fetching**: Fetch sub-issues only when expanded
4. **Visual hierarchy**: Show sub-issues indented with metadata
5. **Refresh support**: g key to refresh epic status

## Example Output (expanded)

```
○ beads.el-7bea Update beads.el for beads 0.21.5 compatibility
   Progress: 3/17 children closed (17%)

○ bde-1 beads.el: Magit-like Emacs interface for Beads
   Progress: 3/11 children closed (27%)
     beads.el-n9h [P3] [task] closed - Add --json global flag
     beads.el-b63 [P3] [task] closed - Add --db global flag
     beads.el-2fz [P3] [task] closed - Add --actor global flag

○ bde-57 Epic: Integration tests for WORKFLOW.md scenarios
   Progress: 0/6 children closed (0%)
```

## User Benefits

- Quick overview of all epics and their progress
- Fast navigation to epic sub-issues
- Expand/collapse for focused viewing
- No need to run separate bd commands to see children
- Integrated with beads.el workflow
title: Add beads-epic-status command with interactive sub-issue display

description: Research the with-simulated-input package to determine if it can be used for testing transient menus in batch mode. The package claims to provide a macro that evaluates forms while simulating input events, reimplemented using execute-kbd-macro to work in batch mode.
notes:
Research findings:

**with-simulated-input Package**:
- Available on MELPA/NonGNU ELPA
- Designed for non-interactive testing of interactive functions
- Works in batch mode by using execute-kbd-macro internally
- Can mix key sequences and Lisp code: (with-simulated-input ("key" (do-something) "RET"))

**Critical Issue for Transient Testing** (magit/transient#180):
- Transients fail in batch mode when called with funcall
- Solution: Must use call-interactively to trigger pre/post-command-hooks
- Hooks are essential for transient state management

**Recommendation**:
NOT viable for our integration testing needs because:
1. Transients require interactive event loop
2. Our current approach (mocking transient-args + call-interactively) is simpler and more reliable
3. with-simulated-input adds complexity without clear benefits for suffix command testing

**Alternative**: Continue with current integration test pattern using beads-test-with-transient-args macro
title: Research and evaluate with-simulated-input package for transient testing

description:
Implement transient menu for updating existing issues.

Transient definition:
- Prefix: beads.el-update (bound in main menu and show buffer)
- Infixes for all flags:
  - Status (-s): open, in_progress, blocked, closed
  - Priority (-p): 0-4
  - Type (--type): bug, feature, task, epic, chore
  - Title (--title): change title
  - Acceptance criteria (--acceptance-criteria): multiline
  - Design (--design): multiline
  - Notes (--notes): multiline
  - Assignee (-a): set assignee
  - External ref (--external-ref): gh-N, jira-X, etc.
- Suffix: Execute update command

Features:
- Auto-populate current values from issue
- Support updating from list buffer (use current item)
- Support updating from show buffer (use current issue)
- Show diff of changes before applying
- Auto-refresh buffers after update

Context-aware:
- In list buffer: operate on current item or marked items
- In show buffer: operate on displayed issue
- Elsewhere: prompt for issue ID
title: beads.el: Transient menu for bd update command

description:
Implement the 'bd label add' command in the beads-label.el module.

## Command Signature
```
bd label add [issue-id...] [label]
```

## Features

- Add a single label to one or more issues
- Support batch operations (multiple issue IDs)
- Context-aware: detect current issue from beads-list or beads-show buffer
- Use beads--label-completion-table for label name auto-completion
- Use beads--issue-completion-table for issue ID completion
- Display success message with count of issues labeled
- Invalidate label cache after successful add (beads--invalidate-label-cache)

## Implementation

1. State variables:
   - beads-label-add--issue-ids (comma-separated or from context)
   - beads-label-add--label-name

2. Reader functions:
   - beads-label-add-read-issue-ids (with completion, context-aware)
   - beads-label-add-read-label (with completion from existing labels)

3. Transient menu:
   - Infix for issue IDs (required)
   - Infix for label name (required)
   - Suffix to execute add command
   - Suffix to preview command
   - Suffix to reset state

4. Execution:
   - Build command: label add [issue-ids...] [label]
   - Execute via beads--run-command
   - Parse JSON result
   - Display success message
   - Invalidate label cache
   - Refresh current buffer if in list/show mode

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Transient menu beads-label-add defined
- [ ] Support single and multiple issue IDs
- [ ] Label name auto-completion from existing labels
- [ ] Context detection from beads-list and beads-show buffers
- [ ] Success message shows number of issues labeled
- [ ] Label cache invalidated after add
- [ ] ERT tests in test/beads-label-test.el
- [ ] Autoload entry in beads.el
- [ ] Integration tests with :tags '(integration)
title: beads-label.el: Implement bd label add command


- [ ] Agent buffers show header-line with context

- [ ] Worktree path shown (abbreviated)

   - ID column shows issue IDs (e.g., my-project-1, my-project-2)

description:
Add integration tests for directory-bound agent sessions.

## Purpose
Ensure directory-bound agent behavior works correctly.

## Test Files
- New: `lisp/test/beads-agent-project-test.el`
- Modified: `lisp/test/beads-agent-test.el`

## Critical Tests

### Session Identity
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-session-keyed-by-project ()
  "Session identity is project directory, not issue."
  ;; Start agent in project
  ;; Verify session ID is project-name#N
  ;; Verify project-dir is set correctly
  ...)

(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-same-project-reuses-lookup ()
  "Multiple starts in same project increment instance number."
  ;; Start agent 1 → beads.el#1
  ;; Start agent 2 → beads.el#2
  ;; Both in same project session
  ...)
```

### Focus Switching
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-focus-switch ()
  "Agent can switch focus between issues."
  ;; Start with focus on bd-42
  ;; Switch focus to bd-43
  ;; Verify current-issue changed
  ;; Verify both in touched-issues
  ...)

(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-session-persists-across-issues ()
  "Session survives issue close/switch."
  ;; Start focused on bd-42
  ;; Close bd-42 (in beads, not agent)
  ;; Session still active
  ;; Can focus on bd-43
  ...)
```

### Work Tracking
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-touched-issues-tracked ()
  "All focused issues are tracked in touched-issues."
  ;; Start with bd-42
  ;; Focus bd-43
  ;; Focus bd-44
  ;; touched-issues = (bd-42 bd-43 bd-44)
  ...)

(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-query-sessions-touching-issue ()
  "Can find sessions that touched an issue."
  ;; Start session, focus bd-42
  ;; Query sessions touching bd-42
  ;; Returns our session
  ...)
```

### Backward Compatibility
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-agent-test-start-with-issue-backward-compat ()
  "Starting with issue sets it as initial focus."
  ;; Old-style: (beads-agent-start "bd-42")
  ;; Verify current-issue = bd-42
  ;; Verify touched-issues = (bd-42)
  ...)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session identity tests
- [ ] Focus switching tests
- [ ] Work tracking tests
- [ ] Backward compatibility tests
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Tests are deterministic
- [ ] Proper mocking (no real bd commands)
title: Add integration tests for directory-bound agents

description: Document the integration testing patterns, best practices, and templates developed during this epic so other developers can easily add tests for new transient menus.
notes:
Document beads-test-interact pattern:

The beads-test-interact function (lisp/test/beads-test.el:74) is a key utility for testing transient menus. It executes keyboard macro commands while preserving default-directory, avoiding issues with execute-kbd-macro.

Usage pattern:
  (beads-test-interact '("t Title RET" "x"))

This simulates typing 't', then 'Title', then RET, then 'x' - perfect for testing transient menu interactions.

Key characteristics:
- Preserves default-directory (unlike execute-kbd-macro)
- Executes pre-command-hook and post-command-hook for realistic simulation
- Handles undo-boundary correctly
- Works with call-interactively to trigger proper transient hooks

Examples in codebase:
- beads-list-test.el:1002 - Quit transient with 'q'
- beads-list-test.el:1023 - Execute list command with 'x'
- beads-create-test.el:275 - Set title and execute
- beads-create-test.el:309-314 - Full workflow with all fields

See StackOverflow reference in beads-list-test.el:983 for original inspiration.

---

Document beads-test-with-project pattern:

The beads-test-with-project macro (lisp/test/beads-test.el:41) creates a temporary beads project for integration testing. Essential for tests that need a real bd database.

Usage patterns:
  (beads-test-with-project ()
    ;; test code with default project settings
    )

  (beads-test-with-project (:prefix "myproject" :quiet t)
    ;; test code with custom init args
    )

What it does:
- Creates temporary directory with make-temp-file
- Initializes beads project via beads-command-init
- Sets default-directory to the temp project
- Clears beads--project-cache to avoid cross-test pollution
- Mocks beads--find-project-root to return nil (forces use of temp dir instead of discovering main repo)

Key benefits:
- Provides isolated test environment with real bd database
- No need to mock bd command execution
- Tests actual bd CLI behavior and JSON output
- Automatic cleanup via temp directory
- Prevents tests from interfering with each other

Usage in integration tests:
- beads-delete-test.el:272 - Full delete workflow
- beads-export-test.el:81 - Export command testing
- beads-create-test.el:300 - Full creation workflow with all fields
- Used in almost every :integration tagged test

Common pattern:
  (skip-unless (executable-find beads-executable))
  (beads-test-with-project ()
    ;; Create test data
    (let ((issue (beads-command-create! :title "Test")))
      ;; Test operations on the data
      ...))

description:
## Problem

No way to bulk-start agents for multiple issues at once.

## Solution

Add `beads-agent-spawn-parallel` command:

```elisp
(defun beads-agent-spawn-parallel (issue-ids)
  "Spawn agents for multiple ISSUE-IDS in parallel.
Each agent gets its own worktree. Non-blocking."
  (interactive (list (beads-completion-read-issues "Spawn for issues: ")))
  (dolist (id issue-ids)
    (beads-agent-start id nil nil "Task")))
```

## Implementation

1. Add multi-select issue completion to `beads-completion.el`
2. Create `beads-agent-spawn-parallel` command
3. Add to agent transient menu
4. Consider progress reporting for bulk operations

## Files to Modify

- `lisp/beads-agent.el`
- `lisp/beads-completion.el` - add multi-select support

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Multi-select issue completion works
- [ ] Command spawns agents for all selected issues
- [ ] Each agent gets its own worktree
- [ ] Non-blocking operation
- [ ] Available in transient menu
- [ ] Progress feedback for bulk operations

description:
Apply the buffer naming infrastructure from beads.el-2pgs to all agent backends.

Each backend needs to either:
1. Accept a buffer-name parameter and use it when creating buffers
2. Rename the buffer after creation to match the beads format

## Format
`*beads-agent[ISSUE-ID][TYPE#N]*`

## Backends to update
- [ ] mock - Use buffer-name parameter directly
- [ ] claude-code-ide - Rename after creation (upstream control)
- [ ] claude-code - Rename after creation (upstream control)  
- [ ] claudemacs - Rename after creation (upstream control)
- [ ] agent-shell - Use buffer-name parameter
- [ ] efrit - Rename after creation (upstream control)

## Implementation Strategy
1. Add :buffer-name keyword to beads-agent-backend-start protocol
2. Mock and agent-shell can use it directly
3. Upstream backends (claude-code-ide, etc.) need post-creation rename
4. Store original buffer name for backend lookup
5. Update beads-agent--start-backend-async to pass buffer name

## Files to modify
- lisp/beads-agent-backend.el - Protocol extension
- lisp/beads-agent.el - Pass buffer name to backends
- lisp/beads-agent-mock.el - Accept buffer-name
- lisp/beads-agent-agent-shell.el - Accept buffer-name
- lisp/beads-agent-claude-code-ide.el - Rename after creation
- lisp/beads-agent-claude-code.el - Rename after creation
- lisp/beads-agent-claudemacs.el - Rename after creation
- lisp/beads-agent-efrit.el - Rename after creation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Excellent test coverage for buffer naming integration
- [ ] E2E testing with new Emacs server and emacsclient simulating user creating agents
- [ ] E2E tests cover ALL backends:
  - [ ] mock
  - [ ] claude-code-ide
  - [ ] claude-code
  - [ ] claudemacs
  - [ ] agent-shell
  - [ ] efrit
- [ ] All backends correctly rename/name buffers in the format `*beads-agent[ISSUE-ID][TYPE#N]*`

description:
## Problem

After stopping all agents (including multiple agents per issue), the issue list still shows agent indicators like "T" or "T#1" next to issues:

```
myproject-047      task       open              P2 T               Build text-file package
myproject-83d      task       open              P2 T#1             Build simple-script package
myproject-xot      task       open              P2                 Build hello-world package
```

## Expected Behavior

When all agents are stopped, no agent indicators should appear in the issue list. The indicators should only show for issues with currently running agents.

## Likely Cause

The agent indicator display logic may be reading from:
1. Stale session data that wasn't cleaned up properly
2. The `beads-agent--issue-outcomes` hash table which stores finished/failed outcomes
3. The sesman session registry not being properly cleared

## Investigation Areas

- `beads-list.el` - How agent indicators are rendered
- `beads-agent-backend.el` - Session cleanup on stop
- `beads-agent--issue-outcomes` - Outcome tracking that persists after stop
title: Agent indicators persist in issue list after stopping all agents

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-completion.el:38-48` - `beads-completion--get-cached-issues`

## Problem
On command failure, cache is set to nil and nil is returned:

```elisp
(condition-case nil
    (setq beads-completion--cache
          (cons now (beads-command-list!)))
  (error (setq beads-completion--cache nil)))  ;; Cache cleared!
(cdr beads-completion--cache)  ;; Returns nil
```

## Impact
Users see "no issues found" when the real problem is a command failure. Better to show stale data with warning than nothing.

## Fix Options
1. Keep previous cached values on error
2. Return error indicator instead of nil
3. Show user-visible error message

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Previous cache preserved on error
- [ ] User informed of fetch failure
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix cache returns nil on error instead of stale data

description:
Allow users to define custom agent types.

## Implementation

### Via defcustom
```elisp
(defcustom beads-agent-custom-types nil
  "List of custom agent type definitions.
Each element: (NAME LETTER PROMPT-TEMPLATE)"
  :type '(repeat (list string string string)))
```

### Via EIEIO subclassing
Document how users can create custom types:
```elisp
(defclass my-agent-type (beads-agent-type)
  ((name :initform "my-type")
   (letter :initform "M")
   (prompt-template :initform "...")))
(beads-agent-type-register (my-agent-type))
```

### Registration
- Add function to convert defcustom entries to agent types
- Auto-register on load
- Validate letter uniqueness
notes:
## Comprehensive Acceptance Criteria

### Unit Tests - Defcustom Approach
- [ ] **Variable Exists**: beads-agent-custom-types defined
- [ ] **Type Correct**: Accepts list of (NAME LETTER PROMPT)
- [ ] **Docstring**: Comprehensive documentation
- [ ] **Empty Default**: Starts as nil or empty list

### Unit Tests - EIEIO Subclassing
- [ ] **Subclass Works**: Can define (defclass my-type (beads-agent-type) ...)
- [ ] **Slots Override**: name, letter, prompt-template, description settable
- [ ] **Methods Work**: Generic functions dispatch to subclass
- [ ] **Registration**: beads-agent-type-register accepts instances

### Unit Tests - Letter Uniqueness
- [ ] **Unique Check**: Registration fails on duplicate letter
- [ ] **Case Sensitive**: T and t are different (or same?)
- [ ] **Error Message**: Clear message about conflict
- [ ] **Reserved Letters**: T/R/P/Q/C reserved for built-ins

### Mock Tests
- [ ] **Mock Registry**: Isolated registry for testing
- [ ] **Reset Between Tests**: Custom types don't leak

### Defcustom Loading Tests
- [ ] **Convert Function**: beads-agent-type--load-custom-types
- [ ] **Auto-Register**: Types registered when variable set
- [ ] **Reload Works**: Changing variable updates registry
- [ ] **Remove Works**: Deleting from list unregisters?

### Emacs Client Tests
- [ ] **Customize Interface**: Types editable via customize
- [ ] **Validate Input**: Invalid entries caught early
- [ ] **Completion**: Custom types appear in type completion

### Integration Tests
- [ ] **Define and Use**: Set defcustom → verify in registry → start agent
- [ ] **Subclass and Use**: Define class → register → start agent
- [ ] **Persist Across Sessions**: setq-default works

### Documentation Tests
- [ ] **Docstring Example**: Shows defcustom format
- [ ] **Commentary Example**: Shows EIEIO approach
- [ ] **README Section**: Documents custom types

### Quality Gates
- [ ] >90% test coverage for custom type support
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Byte-compilation passes

---
**Note**: The `requires-plan-mode` slot is being removed from the base class - see beads.el-ib4x. Custom types no longer need to consider plan mode compatibility as all backends support all types uniformly through prompt-based behavior.
title: Add user-defined agent type support

description:
## Problem

Undercover.el (code coverage tool) fails to instrument files that use `transient-define-prefix` and related transient macros. This happens because undercover uses edebug for instrumentation, and edebug fails to parse the transient macro syntax.

Error from CI logs:
```
UNDERCOVER: Error while loading .../lisp/beads-close.el for coverage:
UNDERCOVER: Invalid read syntax: "Failed matching", ("transient-define-prefix" name lambda-list [&optional lambda-doc] [&rest keywordp sexp] [&rest vectorp] [&optional ("interactive" interactive) def-body])
UNDERCOVER: The problem may be due to edebug failing to parse the file.
```

## Affected Files

The following files contain `transient-define-prefix` and are currently excluded from coverage:
- lisp/beads-close.el
- lisp/beads-create.el
- lisp/beads-dep.el
- lisp/beads-export.el
- lisp/beads-import.el
- lisp/beads-init.el
- lisp/beads-label.el
- lisp/beads-list.el
- lisp/beads-main.el
- lisp/beads-option.el
- lisp/beads-reopen.el
- lisp/beads-sync.el
- lisp/beads-update.el

## Root Cause

The transient library's edebug specs may not be loaded when undercover tries to instrument the files. Undercover uses `edebug-all-defs` mode to instrument code, which requires proper edebug specs for all macros.

## Possible Solutions

1. **Load transient edebug specs before instrumentation**: Ensure transient library is required before undercover starts instrumenting, so edebug specs are available.

2. **Configure undercover to require transient first**: Add transient to the undercover setup in Eldev so it's loaded before instrumentation.

3. **Use undercover's env parameter**: Configure which libraries must be loaded before instrumentation.

4. **Alternative coverage tool**: Consider buttercup-coverage or another tool if undercover cannot be fixed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All tests pass (1156/1156)
- [ ] Code coverage includes ALL files in lisp/*.el (excluding lisp/test/*.el)
- [ ] Coverage report shows non-zero coverage for all 13 files listed above
- [ ] Coverage workflow succeeds on GitHub Actions
notes:
## Root Cause Analysis

The issue was caused by a mismatch between transient's edebug specs and how beads.el uses transient macros.

### Problem Details

1. **Undercover uses edebug for instrumentation**: When undercover.el instruments files for coverage, it uses edebug with `edebug-all-defs` mode.

2. **Transient's edebug spec is incomplete**: The edebug spec for `transient-define-prefix` uses `[&rest vectorp]` which only matches vectors:
   ```elisp
   (debug ( &define name lambda-list
            [&optional lambda-doc]
            [&rest keywordp sexp]
            [&rest vectorp]  ; <-- Only matches vectors!
            ...))
   ```

3. **beads.el uses symbol references for groups**: We use `transient-define-group` to define reusable groups and include them by symbol reference:
   ```elisp
   (transient-define-prefix beads-close--menu ()
     ["Close Issue" ...]
     beads-option-global-section  ; <-- Symbol, not vector!
     ["Actions" ...])
   ```

4. **The spec doesn't account for symbol references**: While transient supports including groups by symbol (documented feature), the edebug spec doesn't match this syntax, causing "Failed matching" errors.

## Solution

Override transient's edebug specs in Eldev to accept both vectors AND symbols using `[&rest [&or symbolp vectorp]]`:

```elisp
(add-hook 'eldev-test-hook
          (lambda ()
            (require 'transient)
            (def-edebug-spec transient-define-prefix
              (&define name lambda-list
                       [&optional lambda-doc]
                       [&rest keywordp sexp]
                       [&rest [&or symbolp vectorp]]
                       [&optional ("interactive" interactive) def-body]))
            (def-edebug-spec transient-define-group
              (&define name [&rest [&or symbolp vectorp]]))))
```

## Results

- All 1156 tests pass
- Coverage now includes 24 files with executable code
- 3 files (beads-custom.el, beads-error.el, beads-state.el) correctly excluded as they contain only declarations (defvar, defcustom, define-error) with no functions to instrument
- All quality checks pass (test, lint, compile)

## Files Changed

- `Eldev`: Added edebug spec overrides in eldev-test-hook
title: Fix code coverage for files using transient macros

The renumber command renumbers all issues sequentially to eliminate ID
gaps (e.g., after deleting issues).

- Strong confirmation prompt showing:
  - Number of issues to be renumbered
  - Impact (references in other issues, git history, etc.)
- Display results (number of issues renumbered)
- Invalidate all caches after renumber
