description:
Create the main user-facing commands for starting agents by type.

## Implementation
In lisp/beads-agent.el, add commands:
- `beads-agent-start-task`
- `beads-agent-start-review`
- `beads-agent-start-plan`
- `beads-agent-start-qa`
- `beads-agent-start-custom`

Each command should:
1. Get issue at point (works in beads-list-mode and beads-show-mode)
2. If no C-u prefix AND active agent exists for issue → jump to its buffer
3. Otherwise, start new agent with type's preferred backend
notes:
## Comprehensive Acceptance Criteria

### Unit Tests - Each Command
- [ ] **beads-agent-start-task**: Exists, documented, autoloaded
- [ ] **beads-agent-start-review**: Exists, documented, autoloaded
- [ ] **beads-agent-start-plan**: Exists, documented, autoloaded
- [ ] **beads-agent-start-qa**: Exists, documented, autoloaded
- [ ] **beads-agent-start-custom**: Exists, documented, autoloaded

### Unit Tests - Jump-or-Start Logic
- [ ] **No Prefix + No Agent**: Starts new agent
- [ ] **No Prefix + Has Agent**: Jumps to existing buffer
- [ ] **C-u Prefix + No Agent**: Starts new agent
- [ ] **C-u Prefix + Has Agent**: Forces new agent start

### Mock Tests
- [ ] **Mock Jump**: Verify switch-to-buffer called
- [ ] **Mock Start**: Verify beads-agent-start called with type
- [ ] **Mock Session Lookup**: Control session existence for testing
- [ ] **Mock Issue Context**: Provide issue ID without real buffer

### Context Detection Tests
- [ ] **From beads-list-mode**: Gets issue at point correctly
- [ ] **From beads-show-mode**: Gets issue from buffer name
- [ ] **From Other Buffer**: Error or prompt for issue ID
- [ ] **No Issue at Point**: Clear error message

### Emacs Client Tests
- [ ] **Interactive Spec**: All commands have (interactive)
- [ ] **Keybindable**: Commands work when bound to keys
- [ ] **Which-Key**: Commands have descriptive names
- [ ] **Menu Integration**: Could appear in menu (not required)

### Integration Tests
- [ ] **Start Then Jump**: Start Task → navigate away → T → jumps back
- [ ] **Force New**: Start Task → C-u T → verify two sessions
- [ ] **Type Preserved**: Started type matches session type-name
- [ ] **Backend Selection**: Preferred backend used

### Custom Agent Tests
- [ ] **Prompts User**: read-string called
- [ ] **Empty Prompt**: Handled gracefully (cancel or error)
- [ ] **Prompt Content**: User input becomes agent prompt

### Quality Gates
- [ ] >90% test coverage for all 5 commands
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Byte-compilation passes
title: Add agent type start commands with smart jump-or-start logic

description:
## Goal

Reorganize all beads-command subclasses into separate files matching the logical grouping from `bd help`.

## Current State

- Most commands (23 classes) are in `lisp/beads-command.el`
- A few already extracted: beads-command-blocked.el, beads-command-doctor.el, beads-command-ready.el

## Target Organization

Based on `bd help` grouping:

### 1. beads-command-maintenance.el
- beads-command-doctor (already separate - move here)
- rename-prefix, repair, migrate, preflight, upgrade, worktree

### 2. beads-command-issue.el (Working With Issues)
- beads-command-create
- beads-command-update
- beads-command-close
- beads-command-reopen
- beads-command-delete
- beads-command-show
- beads-command-list
- beads-command-edit
- comments, gate, label, merge-slot, move, q, refile, search, set-state, state

### 3. beads-command-views.el (Views & Reports)
- beads-command-stats
- beads-command-blocked (already separate - move here)
- beads-command-ready (already separate - move here)
- activity, count, lint, stale, status

### 4. beads-command-deps.el (Dependencies & Structure)
- beads-command-dep-add
- beads-command-dep-remove
- beads-command-dep-tree
- beads-command-dep-cycles
- beads-command-epic-close-eligible
- beads-command-epic-status

### 7. beads-command-label.el (Label operations)

## Implementation Notes

description:
## Problem

Current implementation runs `bd init` and `bd import` in each worktree, creating separate databases instead of leveraging beads' shared database architecture.

## Current Code (`lisp/beads-agent.el:345-392`)

```elisp
(defun beads-agent--init-worktree-beads-async (issue-id worktree-path callback)
  ;; Runs bd init in worktree <- WRONG
  ;; Runs bd import from main repo <- WRONG
  ...)
```

## Upstream Behavior (from WORKTREES.md)

> "Worktrees automatically share the database"
> "One database - All worktrees share the same `.beads` directory in main repo"
> `bd ready  # Works immediately - sees same issues`

## Fix Required

1. Remove `bd init` call from `beads-agent--init-worktree-beads-async`
2. Remove `beads-agent--import-worktree-issues-async` function entirely
3. Remove `beads-agent--extract-prefix` function (no longer needed)
4. Keep `BD_NO_DAEMON=1` environment setting (correct for worktree safety)
5. Simplify to just verify worktree creation succeeded

## Files to Modify

- `lisp/beads-agent.el` (lines 345-422)

## Tests to Update

- `lisp/test/beads-agent-test.el` - update worktree creation tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No `bd init` called in worktrees
- [ ] No `bd import` called in worktrees
- [ ] Worktrees use shared database from main repo
- [ ] `BD_NO_DAEMON=1` still set for worktree safety
- [ ] All existing tests pass or are updated
title: Fix Worktree Shared Database Support

description:
Integrate directory-bound agent sessions with project-level sessions.

## Key Change
Agent sessions are now keyed by project directory, not issue. They integrate naturally with project sessions.

## Purpose
Track agent sessions within their parent project session for lifecycle management. Both share the same identity: project directory.

## Files to Modify
- `beads-agent.el` - agent start/stop hooks
- `beads-sesman.el` - agent tracking in project session

## Integration Points

### On Agent Start
```elisp
(when-let ((project-session (beads-sesman--ensure-project-session)))
  (cl-pushnew agent-session (oref project-session agent-sessions)))
```

### On Agent Stop
```elisp
(when-let ((project-session (beads-sesman--current-project-session)))
  (setf (oref project-session agent-sessions)
        (delq agent-session (oref project-session agent-sessions)))
  (beads-sesman--maybe-cleanup-session project-session))
```

## Session Relationship
```
Project Session (project-dir)
├── buffers: [*beads-list*, *beads-show: bd-42*]
└── agent-sessions: [beads.el#1, beads.el#2]
        ├── current-issue: bd-42
        └── touched-issues: [bd-42, bd-43]
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### Agent Registration
- [ ] Agent added to project session on start
- [ ] Uses project-dir as lookup key
- [ ] Multiple agents in same project session

### Agent Unregistration
- [ ] Agent removed on stop
- [ ] Cleanup via state-change-hook
- [ ] Session preserved while any agent running

### Directory-Based Lookup
- [ ] `beads-agent--get-sessions-for-project(project-dir)`
- [ ] Returns all agent sessions in that project
- [ ] NOT per-issue lookup (deprecated)

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Agent start before any buffers exist
- [ ] Agent stop after all buffers killed
- [ ] Multiple agents in same project

### Testing
- [ ] Test: agent registration with project session
- [ ] Test: multiple agents same project
- [ ] Test: session cleanup logic

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings
- [ ] Passes lint checks
- [ ] All existing agent tests pass
title: Integrate directory-bound agent sessions with project sessions

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-agent.el:769-778` - `beads-agent--rename-and-store-buffer`

## Observation
Possible (unlikely) race between `buffer-live-p` check and `rename-buffer`:
```elisp
(when (and buffer (buffer-live-p buffer))
  ;; Time window where buffer could be killed here
  (with-current-buffer buffer
    (rename-buffer new-name t)))
```

## Fix
Move check inside with-current-buffer or use condition-case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Defensive check in place
- [ ] Test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Add buffer-live-p check before rename

description:
Update agent naming to use project directory as identity, not issue ID.

## Key Change
**Directory is identity, issue is current-focus metadata.**

Agent sessions are identified by project context, not by which issue they work on.

## Current State (Issue-Bound)
```
Session ID: bde-42#1
Buffer: *beads-agent[bde-42][Task#1]*
Sesman: bde-42#1@~/projects/beads.el
```

## New State (Directory-Bound)
```
Session ID: beads.el#1
Buffer: *beads-agent[beads.el][#1]*
Sesman: beads.el#1
```

## Files to Modify
- `beads-agent-backend.el` - session ID generation, buffer naming
- `beads-sesman.el` - session naming for sesman

## Functions to Change

### Session ID Generation
```elisp
(defun beads-agent--generate-session-id (project-dir)
  "Generate session ID from project directory.
Returns `project-name#N' format (e.g., beads.el#1)."
  (let* ((project-name (beads-agent--derive-project-name project-dir))
         (next-num (beads-agent--next-instance-number project-dir)))
    (format "%s#%d" project-name next-num)))
```

### Buffer Naming
```elisp
(defun beads-agent--generate-buffer-name (session)
  "Generate buffer name: *beads-agent[project-name][#N]*"
  (format "*beads-agent[%s][#%d]*"
          (oref session project-name)
          (oref session instance-number)))
```

### Instance Numbering
- Key by `project-dir` (not `(issue-id . type-name)`)
- Remove per-issue instance tracking

## Acceptance Criteria

### Session ID Format
- [ ] Format: `project-name#N` (e.g., `beads.el#1`)
- [ ] Instance number unique per project
- [ ] Issue ID NOT in session ID

### Buffer Naming Format
- [ ] Format: `*beads-agent[project-name][#N]*`
- [ ] Issue ID NOT in buffer name
- [ ] Project name for disambiguation

### Sesman Naming
- [ ] Session name: `project-name#N`
- [ ] NOT `issue-id#N@path`

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Works in regular git repos
- [ ] Works in git worktrees
- [ ] Handles same project name in different paths (disambiguation)
- [ ] Works over Tramp

### Testing
- [ ] Test: session ID format
- [ ] Test: buffer name format
- [ ] Test: instance numbering per project

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings
- [ ] Passes lint checks
- [ ] All existing agent tests updated
title: Update agent buffer/session naming for directory-bound identity

description:
## Problem
beads.el shows issues from the main git repository instead of the worktree when the worktree has its own `.beads` directory.

**Root cause**: `beads--find-beads-dir` uses `beads--find-project-root` which may return the main repo path instead of the worktree path.

## Changes

### 1. Fix beads--find-beads-dir (lisp/beads.el:258-260)
Remove `(beads--find-project-root)` from the `or` chain. Start from `default-directory` to find worktree-local `.beads` first.

### 2. Change beads-worktree-auto-no-daemon default (lisp/beads-custom.el:100)
Change default from `nil` to `t`.

### 3. Remove warning machinery (simplification)
Since auto-no-daemon now defaults to t, warning is redundant.
- Remove `beads-worktree-warn-daemon` defcustom
- Remove `beads--worktree-warned` variable
- Remove `beads--maybe-warn-worktree` function
- Remove call site in beads-main.el

### 4. Update tests

- Remove warning-related tests

## Files to Modify
- lisp/beads.el
- lisp/beads-custom.el
- lisp/beads-main.el
- lisp/test/beads-worktree-test.el
title: Fix worktree support: prefer local .beads, auto --no-daemon

description:
Update beads-list.el to use project directory as buffer identity.

## Key Change
**Directory is identity, branch is metadata.**

Buffer lookup is now by project-dir, not by buffer name with branch suffix.

## Buffer-Local Variables
```elisp
(defvar-local beads-list--project-dir nil
  "Project root directory for this buffer. THIS IS THE IDENTITY.")

(defvar-local beads-list--branch nil
  "Git branch name. This is METADATA for display, not identity.")

(defvar-local beads-list--project-name nil
  "Project name for buffer disambiguation.")
```

## Functions to Add

### Buffer Lookup by Project
```elisp
(defun beads-list--find-buffer-for-project (buffer-type project-dir)
  "Find existing buffer for BUFFER-TYPE and PROJECT-DIR."
  (cl-find-if
   (lambda (buf)
     (with-current-buffer buf
       (and (derived-mode-p 'beads-list-mode)
            (equal beads-list--project-dir project-dir)
            (string-match-p (format "beads-%s" buffer-type)
                           (buffer-name buf)))))
   (buffer-list)))
```

### Buffer Get-or-Create
```elisp
(defun beads-list--get-or-create-buffer (buffer-type)
  "Get or create list buffer for current project context.
Reuses existing buffer for same project-dir."
  (let* ((project-dir (or (beads--find-project-root) default-directory))
         (existing (beads-list--find-buffer-for-project buffer-type project-dir)))
    (or existing
        (let ((buffer (get-buffer-create (format "*beads-%s*" buffer-type))))
          (with-current-buffer buffer
            (setq beads-list--project-dir project-dir)
            (setq beads-list--branch (beads--get-git-branch))
            (setq beads-list--project-name (beads--get-project-name)))
          buffer))))
```

## Functions to Modify
- `beads-list--transient-execute` (line ~558) - use get-or-create-buffer
- `beads-ready` (line ~1079) - use get-or-create-buffer
- `beads-blocked` (line ~1113) - use get-or-create-buffer

## Acceptance Criteria

### Buffer Identity by Directory
- [ ] Buffer lookup uses project-dir, not buffer name
- [ ] Same project-dir → reuses existing buffer
- [ ] Different project-dir → creates new buffer
- [ ] **Branch switch in same dir → SAME buffer** (critical\!)

### Buffer-Local State
- [ ] `beads-list--project-dir` set on creation (identity)
- [ ] `beads-list--branch` set on creation (metadata)
- [ ] `beads-list--project-name` set on creation
- [ ] State preserved across refresh

### Buffer Naming
- [ ] Simple name `*beads-list*` for single project
- [ ] Branch NOT in buffer name
- [ ] Future: disambiguation for multiple projects

### Context Preservation
- [ ] Refresh uses stored project-dir
- [ ] Commands run in correct default-directory
- [ ] Refresh updates branch metadata (in case it changed)

### Testing
- [ ] Test: same dir, different branch → same buffer
- [ ] Test: different dir → different buffer
- [ ] Test: refresh preserves project-dir
- [ ] Test: refresh updates branch metadata

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings
- [ ] Passes lint checks
- [ ] All existing tests pass
title: Update list buffer for directory-aware identity

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the bd compact command.

The compact command compacts old closed issues using semantic
summarization to save space. It has two tiers:
- Tier 1: Issues closed 30+ days ago
- Tier 2: Issues closed 90+ days ago

Features:
- Transient menu for compact command
- Show preview of issues to be compacted before execution
- Confirmation prompt with tier information
- Display results (number of issues compacted per tier)
- Refresh list buffers after compacting

This is a destructive operation that should require explicit user
confirmation.
title: beads.el: Implement bd compact command

`lisp/beads-show.el:327-345` - `beads-show--get-sub-issues`

## Problem
Returns nil on any error; can't distinguish "no sub-issues" from "fetch failed":
```elisp
(condition-case nil
    ...
  (error nil))  ;; Same as empty result!
```

Return distinct values or show error indicator.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Distinct return for error vs empty
- [ ] User can see when fetch failed
- [ ] Test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux

description:
Add a customization option to control whether unavailable backends are shown
in the completion list.

## Why

Currently, all backends (available and unavailable) are shown in completions
with unavailable ones grayed out and filtered by the predicate. Some users may
prefer to only see available backends to reduce noise, while others appreciate
seeing all options for awareness.

## Implementation

1. Add defcustom `beads-completion-show-unavailable-backends` (default t)
2. Update `beads-completion-backend-table` to respect this setting
3. Update `beads-agent--select-backend` to respect this setting
4. Add tests for both configurations

## Proposed API

```elisp
(defcustom beads-completion-show-unavailable-backends t
  "Whether to show unavailable backends in completion lists.
When non-nil, unavailable backends are shown but cannot be selected.
When nil, only available backends appear in the completion list."
  :type 'boolean
  :group 'beads-agent)
```

## Behavior

- `t` (default): Show all backends, unavailable ones grayed out, cannot be selected
- `nil`: Only show available backends in the list

## Files to Modify

1. lisp/beads-custom.el - Add defcustom
2. lisp/beads-completion.el - Filter backends based on setting
3. lisp/beads-agent.el - Update beads-agent--select-backend
4. test/beads-completion-test.el - Add tests for both modes
title: Make showing unavailable backends in completions customizable

description: Based on research from task 1, implement full transient menu interaction tests if with-simulated-input proves viable. Otherwise, create comprehensive manual testing checklist.
notes:
# COMPLETED: Full UI Testing with execute-kbd-macro

## Summary

Successfully implemented and documented full transient UI testing using `execute-kbd-macro`. This approach enables true end-to-end integration testing of transient menus in batch mode.

## Key Findings

### ✅ What Works

**Pattern: Combined key + input in single macro**
```elisp
(funcall-interactively #'beads-create)
(execute-kbd-macro (kbd "t Minimal SPC Test RET"))  ; ✅
(execute-kbd-macro (kbd "- t feature RET"))         ; ✅
(execute-kbd-macro (kbd "x"))                        ; ✅
```

### ❌ What Doesn't Work

**Pattern: Splitting key and input**
```elisp
(execute-kbd-macro (kbd "t"))              ; ❌ Opens prompt
(execute-kbd-macro (kbd "Title RET"))      ; ❌ Fails - interprets as transient keys
```

## Test Results

All 3 proof-of-concept tests passing:
- ✅ beads-create-test-full-ui-interaction-kbd-macro (2.3s)
- ✅ beads-create-test-full-ui-interaction-simplified (2.4s)  
- ✅ beads-create-test-full-ui-interaction-title-only (2.1s)

## Performance Analysis

**Overhead: ~0.65s per test**
- Transient infrastructure: ~0.15s
- Multiple kbd macro calls: ~0.20s
- Minibuffer interactions: ~0.20s
- Display refreshes: ~0.10s

This is acceptable for integration testing!

## Comparison: Mocked vs Full UI

### Mocked Approach (1.5s)
- ❌ Skips infix commands
- ❌ Skips transient UI
- ❌ Doesn't test user workflow
- ✅ Simple and fast

### Full UI Approach (2.1s)
- ✅ Tests infix commands
- ✅ Tests transient UI
- ✅ Tests real user workflow
- ✅ No mocking needed
- ✅ Works in batch mode

**Verdict**: +0.65s overhead is worth it for comprehensive coverage!

## Documentation Added

Updated `.claude/skills/emacs-transient/SKILL.md` with:
- Comprehensive testing section
- Two testing approaches comparison
- Critical rules for execute-kbd-macro
- Testing patterns (minimal, full, switches, navigation)
- Helper functions
- When to use each approach
- Complete test suite example
- Debugging tips
- Performance breakdown
- Comparison to casual project and with-simulated-input

## Proof-of-Concept Tests

Added 3 tests to `lisp/test/beads-create-test.el:1482-1576`:
1. Full workflow (all fields)
2. Simplified workflow
3. Minimal workflow (title only)

## Recommendations

1. **Adopt for critical workflows** - Use for key user paths
2. **Keep mocked for unit tests** - Fast tests for suffix logic
3. **At least one UI test per transient** - Verify bindings work
4. **Document in CLAUDE.md** - Add testing guidelines to project

## Research Artifacts

- Casual project analysis (tests bindings only, not execution)
- with-simulated-input evaluation (not needed for our use case)
- Performance benchmarking and overhead analysis
- Working proof-of-concept tests

## Outcome

**MISSION ACCOMPLISHED** 🎯

We now have:
- ✅ Working full UI integration tests
- ✅ Documented patterns and best practices
- ✅ Performance analysis
- ✅ Comprehensive skill documentation
- ✅ Proof-of-concept implementation

The execute-kbd-macro approach is **viable, valuable, and ready for adoption**!
title: Evaluate and implement transient UI tests if feasible

description:
When pressing agent jump keys (e.g., J or P) in the issue list view and there are multiple agents running for the same issue, use completing-read to let the user select which agent buffer to jump to.

Currently, jumping to an agent only considers the first session. With multiple sessions support, users need a way to choose which specific agent buffer to visit.

## Implementation
1. Check if multiple sessions exist for the issue
2. If multiple, present completing-read with session identifiers (e.g., Task#1, Review#2)
3. Jump to the selected session's buffer

## Related
- beads.el-wcvt: Issue list should show multiple agents with numbered format
title: Use completing-read when jumping to agents with multiple sessions

description:
Add comprehensive integration tests for directory-aware buffer system.

## Purpose
Ensure the directory-aware buffer system works correctly. **Critical: branch switch must reuse same buffer.**

## Test Files

### New File: lisp/test/beads-project-test.el
Integration tests for directory-aware buffers.

### Modified: lisp/test/beads-test.el
Unit tests for helper functions.

### Modified: lisp/test/beads-sesman-test.el
Tests for directory-keyed session lifecycle.

## Critical Integration Tests

### CRITICAL: Branch Switch Same Buffer
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-project-test-branch-switch-same-buffer ()
  "Switching branches in same directory reuses buffer."
  ;; Setup: git repo on 'main', open beads-list
  ;; Action: simulate branch switch to 'feature', open beads-list again
  ;; Assert: SAME buffer (not a new one\!)
  ;; Assert: buffer's project-dir unchanged
  ;; Assert: buffer's branch metadata updated to 'feature'
  ...)
```

### Different Directories → Different Buffers
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-project-test-different-dirs-different-buffers ()
  "Different project directories get different buffers."
  ;; Setup: two temp dirs with .beads
  ;; Action: open beads-list from each
  ;; Assert: TWO different buffers
  ;; Assert: each has its own project-dir
  ...)
```

### Same Branch Name, Different Projects
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-project-test-same-branch-different-projects ()
  "Same branch name in different projects gets different buffers."
  ;; Setup: /proj-a on main, /proj-b on main
  ;; Action: open beads-list from each
  ;; Assert: TWO different buffers
  ;; Assert: no collision despite same branch
  ...)
```

## Session Tests

### Directory-Keyed Sessions
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-sesman-test-session-keyed-by-directory ()
  "Session lookup uses directory, not branch."
  ;; Setup: project dir
  ;; Action: create session, simulate branch switch, lookup again
  ;; Assert: SAME session returned
  ...)

(ert-deftest beads-sesman-test-branch-switch-same-session ()
  "Branch switch in same directory keeps same session."
  ;; This is the CRITICAL behavioral change
  ...)
```

## Unit Tests

### Helper Functions
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-test-get-project-name ()
  "Returns basename of project root.")

(ert-deftest beads-test-get-git-branch-is-metadata ()
  "Branch is metadata, documented as such.")

(ert-deftest beads-test-get-worktree-root-is-identity ()
  "Worktree root is identity for sessions.")
```

### Buffer Lookup
```elisp
(ert-deftest beads-list-test-find-buffer-by-project-dir ()
  "Buffer lookup uses project-dir, not buffer name.")

(ert-deftest beads-show-test-find-buffer-by-issue-and-dir ()
  "Show buffer lookup uses (issue-id, project-dir) pair.")
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Branch switch → same buffer (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Different dirs → different buffers
- [ ] Same branch different projects → different buffers
- [ ] Session keyed by directory
- [ ] Session persists through branch switch
- [ ] Helper functions unit tested

### Test Scenarios
- [ ] Regular git repo
- [ ] Git worktree (if testable)
- [ ] No git repo (fallback)
- [ ] Context preservation on refresh
- [ ] Session lifecycle

### Test Quality
- [ ] Tests are deterministic
- [ ] Tests clean up after themselves
- [ ] Tests have clear failure messages

### All Tests Pass
- [ ] All new tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Tests pass in CI

### Quality
- [ ] No byte-compilation warnings in test files
- [ ] Test files pass lint checks
title: Add integration tests for directory-aware buffers

Phase 1: Update beads-agent-backend.el

Add functions for numbered session IDs:
1. `beads-agent--next-session-number` - finds highest existing number for issue, returns N+1
2. Update `beads-agent--generate-session-id` to take issue-id parameter, return `issue-id#N` format

File: lisp/beads-agent-backend.el (lines 294-324)
notes:
## Implementation Plan

### Functions to Add/Modify

1. **`beads-agent--session-number-from-id`** (new helper)
   - Extract session number from ID format `issue-id#N`
   - Returns N as integer, or nil if format doesn't match

2. **`beads-agent--next-session-number`** (new)
   - Find highest existing session number for issue
   - Returns max+1, or 1 if no sessions exist
   - Numbers never reuse (gaps don't matter)

3. **`beads-agent--generate-session-id`** (update)
   - Add issue-id parameter
   - Return `issue-id#N` format instead of UUID

4. **`beads-agent--create-session`** (update)
   - Pass issue-id to generate-session-id

### Test Cases
- Empty case: returns 1 when no sessions
- Existing sessions: returns max+1
- No reuse: gaps in numbering still return max+1
- Per-issue: independent numbering per issue
- Format: validates `issue-id#N` pattern

### Edge Cases
- Special chars in issue-id (safe with #N suffix)
- Backward compat: old UUID format returns nil from helper
title: Add session numbering functions

description:
Implement the 'bd label remove' command in the beads-label.el module.

## Command Signature
```
bd label remove [issue-id...] [label]
```

## Features

- Remove a single label from one or more issues
- Support batch operations (multiple issue IDs)
- Context-aware: detect current issue from beads-list or beads-show buffer
- Use beads--label-completion-table for label name auto-completion
- Use beads--issue-completion-table for issue ID completion
- Display success message with count of issues unlabeled
- Invalidate label cache after successful remove (beads--invalidate-label-cache)
- Handle case where label doesn't exist on issue gracefully

## Implementation

1. State variables:
   - beads-label-remove--issue-ids (comma-separated or from context)
   - beads-label-remove--label-name

2. Reader functions:
   - beads-label-remove-read-issue-ids (with completion, context-aware)
   - beads-label-remove-read-label (with completion from existing labels)

3. Transient menu:
   - Infix for issue IDs (required)
   - Infix for label name (required)
   - Suffix to execute remove command
   - Suffix to preview command
   - Suffix to reset state

4. Execution:
   - Build command: label remove [issue-ids...] [label]
   - Execute via beads--run-command
   - Parse JSON result
   - Display success message
   - Invalidate label cache
   - Refresh current buffer if in list/show mode

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Transient menu beads-label-remove defined
- [ ] Support single and multiple issue IDs
- [ ] Label name auto-completion from existing labels
- [ ] Context detection from beads-list and beads-show buffers
- [ ] Success message shows number of issues unlabeled
- [ ] Label cache invalidated after remove
- [ ] Graceful handling of non-existent labels
- [ ] ERT tests in test/beads-label-test.el
- [ ] Autoload entry in beads.el
- [ ] Integration tests with :tags '(integration)
title: beads-label.el: Implement bd label remove command

description:
Currently beads-create.el has its own validation functions that duplicate
validation logic that should live in the beads-command-create class. This
creates maintenance burden and inconsistency.

## Current State

beads-create.el has duplicate validation:
- beads-create--validate-title
- beads-create--validate-type  
- beads-create--validate-priority
- beads-create--validate-dependencies
- beads-create--validate-all (calls all the above)

beads-command.el already has:
- beads-command-validate method for beads-command-create
- Validates basic constraints (title or file, list types)

## Problem

1. Validation logic is duplicated across two files
2. beads-create.el validates BEFORE calling beads-command-execute
3. beads-command-execute validates AGAIN internally
4. No single source of truth for validation rules
5. beads-update.el already uses the better pattern (calls beads-command-validate)

## Better Pattern (from beads-update.el)

beads-update.el:170 shows the right approach:
```elisp
(defun beads-update--validate-all (cmd)
  "Validate all parameters from CMD command instance."
  (let ((error-msg (beads-command-validate cmd))
        (changes (beads-update--get-changed-fields cmd)))
    (delq nil
          (list
           error-msg
           (when (null changes) "No fields have been changed")))))
```

It:
- Calls beads-command-validate (reuses command class validation)
- Adds only UI-specific validation (e.g., "no fields changed")
title: Refactor beads-create.el validation to use beads-command-validate method

# COMPLETED: Full UI Testing with execute-kbd-macro

## Summary

Successfully implemented and documented full transient UI testing using `execute-kbd-macro`. This approach enables true end-to-end integration testing of transient menus in batch mode.

## Key Findings

### ✅ What Works

**Pattern: Combined key + input in single macro**

```elisp
(funcall-interactively #'beads-create)

```

description:
Implement a comprehensive Emacs transient menu for the 'bd label' command and all its subcommands.

## Overview

The bd label command provides four subcommands for managing issue labels:
- **label add**: Add a label to one or more issues
- **label remove**: Remove a label from one or more issues  
- **label list**: List labels for a specific issue
- **label list-all**: List all unique labels in the database

This epic will create a Magit-like transient interface for all label operations, providing a better UX than typing commands manually.

## Scope

1. Create beads-label.el module with all label functionality
2. Implement parent transient menu (beads-label)
3. Implement transient for each subcommand (add, remove, list, list-all)
4. Context-aware operations (detect current issue from buffer)
5. Display results in dedicated buffers with proper formatting
6. Integration with label completion (from beads.el-jea)
7. Proper cache invalidation after add/remove operations
8. Comprehensive test coverage (unit + integration tests)

## Architecture

Following the pattern established in other beads modules:
- State variables for transient infixes
- Reader functions for input (issue ID, label name)
- Command execution functions calling beads--run-command
- Result display in formatted buffers
- Context detection (list/show mode buffers)
- Batch operations support (multiple issues/labels)

## Integration Points

- Use beads--label-completion-table for label name completion
- Call beads--invalidate-label-cache after add/remove operations
- Update beads-list and beads-show buffers to display labels
- Add menu entry in beads-main.el
- Support global flags (--json, --db, --actor, etc.)

## Benefits

✓ Consistent transient-based UI across all bd commands
✓ Context-aware label operations
✓ Batch label management (add/remove from multiple issues)
✓ Visual feedback with formatted result buffers
✓ Auto-completion for labels and issue IDs
✓ Keyboard-driven workflow (no typing full commands)
title: beads.el: Implement bd label transient menu

description:
Refactor transient menus in beads-misc.el to use the modern transient-args pattern.

**Menus to Update:**
- beads-dep (add/remove dependency commands)
- beads-export (output path)
- beads-import (input path, flags)
- beads-init (prefix, db path)

**Current Pattern:**
- Multiple state variables per menu
- Custom :reader functions
- Manual state management and reset

**New Pattern:**
- Use (transient-args) in execute functions
- Parse arguments to extract values
- Standard transient infixes
- (transient-set) for reset

**Benefits:**
- Consistent with beads-sync.el
- Significantly less code (~150+ lines reduction)
- Standard patterns across all menus
- Easier maintenance

**Reference:**
See beads-sync.el and beads.el-52 for pattern.
title: Refactor beads-misc transient menus to use transient-args pattern

description:
## Root Cause

acp.el creates fire-and-forget timers with `(run-at-time 0 nil ...)` for async message processing. These timers are:
1. **Not tracked** - the timer object is discarded
2. **Not cancelled** on shutdown - `acp-shutdown` only kills process and buffers

## Current Fix (insufficient)

In `beads-agent-backend-stop`:
1. Calls `acp-shutdown` before killing buffer
2. Uses `(sit-for 0)` to let pending timers complete

## Why It Fails

`sit-for 0` doesn't guarantee timers will execute before buffer death. The timer callbacks reference buffers via closure, and when they fire after buffer is killed, Emacs errors with 'Selecting deleted buffer'.

## Proposed Solutions

1. **Increase sit-for delay** to 0.1-0.2 seconds (hacky)
2. **File upstream issue** with acp.el to track and cancel timers
3. **Guard timer callbacks** with `buffer-live-p` checks
notes:
## Fix Attempt #3: Ensure buffer is current when killing

The `agent-shell--clean-up` function checks `(derived-mode-p 'agent-shell-mode)` which requires the buffer being killed to be the current buffer.

Fixed by wrapping the `kill-buffer` call inside `with-current-buffer` so the hook runs with the correct buffer current.

Previous error: "agent-shell--clean-up: Not in a shell"

Now the full cleanup path runs:
1. Buffer is made current via `with-current-buffer`
2. `kill-buffer` triggers the buffer-local `kill-buffer-hook`
3. `agent-shell--clean-up` runs with correct buffer context
4. ACP client, heartbeat, and viewport are properly cleaned up
title: Timer error 'Selecting deleted buffer' after agent-shell buffer killed

description:
## Error

```
Debugger entered--Lisp error: (wrong-type-argument stringp nil)
  claudemacs-setup-bell-handler()
  claudemacs--setup-eat-integration(#<buffer *beads-agent[myproject-047][Plan#4]*>)
  timer-event-handler([t 26950 52113 452342 nil ...])
```

## Analysis

The `beads-agent-claudemacs--advice-setup-bell-handler` only catches `void-function` errors:

```elisp
(condition-case err
    (funcall orig-fun)
  (void-function
   (if (eq (cadr err) '\(setf\ eat-term-parameter\))
       (beads-agent-claudemacs--setup-bell-handler-fixed)
     (signal (car err) (cdr err)))))
```

But the actual error is `wrong-type-argument stringp nil` - a different error type that propagates up.

## Root Cause

The original `claudemacs-setup-bell-handler` is failing with a nil value where a string is expected. This happens in a timer (deferred setup).

## Potential Causes

1. Buffer was renamed by beads before claudemacs completed setup
2. `claudemacs--get-buffer` returns nil or wrong buffer
3. Some claudemacs internal expects original buffer name
notes:
## Fix

Extended the advice around `claudemacs-setup-bell-handler` to catch `wrong-type-argument` errors in addition to `void-function` errors.

When beads renames the buffer from `*claudemacs:...*` to `*beads-agent[...]*`, the claudemacs function `claudemacs--get-current-session-buffer` can't find it and returns nil, causing `(with-current-buffer nil ...)` to error.

Our fallback implementation finds buffers by mode instead of by name pattern, avoiding this issue.
title: Claudemacs startup error: wrong-type-argument stringp nil in setup-bell-handler

notes:
When stopping an agent via the transient menu, the transient remains open.

## Expected behavior
- After stopping an agent, the transient should close automatically
- User returns to their previous buffer/context

## Actual behavior
- Transient stays open after stop action completes

## Fix
The stop suffix likely has `:transient t` or is missing `:transient nil`. Remove the transient property or explicitly set `:transient nil` so it closes after execution.

## Scope: Audit all agent menu suffixes
Review ALL suffixes in the agent transient menu and verify correct transient behavior:

**Should CLOSE after execution (no :transient or :transient nil):**
- Start agent
- Stop agent
- Jump to buffer
- Jump to issue

**Should STAY OPEN (:transient t):**
- Preview commands
- Backend selection (settings)
- Reset state

## Affected component
- beads-agent.el (all transient suffixes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated to cover the fix
- [ ] Fix tested in live Emacs session and confirmed working
- [ ] All existing tests pass
title: Agent transient should close after stopping agent

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-sesman.el:214-227` - `beads-sesman--unregister-session`

## Problem
`beads-sesman--unregister-session` clears buffer-local session ID but doesn't remove the `kill-buffer-hook` that was added during registration (line 212):

```elisp
;; During registration (line 212):
(add-hook 'kill-buffer-hook #'beads-sesman--buffer-kill-handler nil t)

;; During unregistration (line 220-224):
(setq-local beads-sesman--buffer-session-id nil)
;; MISSING: remove-hook!
```

This orphans a hook function that will try to call `beads-agent-stop` when the buffer is eventually killed.

## Impact
Orphaned hook causes unnecessary/incorrect cleanup attempts.

## Fix
Add hook removal:
```elisp
(remove-hook 'kill-buffer-hook #'beads-sesman--buffer-kill-handler t)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook properly removed during unregister
- [ ] No orphaned hooks
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix orphaned kill-buffer-hook in sesman unregister

description:
beads-option.el requires beads-reader.el at module load time while beads-reader.el forward-declares variables from beads-option. This tight coupling could break with loading order changes.

Current structure:
- beads-option.el: (require 'beads-reader)
- beads-reader.el: (defvar beads-option-* ...) ; forward declarations

Risks:
- Fragile loading order
- Potential circular require if beads-reader adds (require 'beads-option)
- Maintenance burden

Solution options:

Option 1: Document the dependency
- Add comments explaining the loading order requirement
- Add note in CLAUDE.md about the dependency structure
- Keep current implementation (simplest)

Option 2: Extract shared state to separate module
- Create beads-state.el for shared variables
- Both modules require beads-state
- Eliminates circular dependency

Option 3: Use autoloads
- Use declare-function and autoload cookies
- Defer loading until runtime
- More complex but more flexible

Recommendation: Start with Option 1 (document), evaluate Options 2-3 if issues arise.
title: Document and potentially resolve circular dependency between beads-option/beads-reader

description:
Similar to the Plan agent prompt improvements (beads.el-6ylh), review and update the prompts for other agent types to ensure they:

1. Update beads issue with results when done
2. Have clear, non-contradictory constraints
3. Use structured output format with heredoc syntax for bd update

## Agent Types to Review

### Task Agent (beads-agent-type-task--prompt)
- Should follow upstream beads task-agent.md pattern
- Must reference issue ID from context
- Should update issue with progress and close when complete

### Review Agent (beads-agent-review-prompt)
- Should update issue with review findings
- Should be read-only for code
- Consider: --notes for review summary

### QA Agent (beads-agent-qa-prompt)
- Should update issue with test results
- Should verify acceptance criteria
- Consider: --notes for test summary, --acceptance for verified criteria

## Reference: Upstream Task Agent

The upstream beads task-agent.md (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/blob/main/.claude-plugin/agents/task-agent.md) provides the canonical workflow pattern. The current beads.el task agent prompt should be updated to match this structure while adding the issue ID reference for bd commands.

## Analysis

The Plan agent prompt (lines 96-154 in beads-agent-types.el) demonstrates good structure with:
- Clear constraints section with read-only + explicit exception
- Structured workflow steps
- Output section with heredoc bd update template
- Issue ID reference from context

The three other agents need similar improvements:
- **Task Agent** (lines 158-192): Update to match upstream task-agent.md + add issue ID reference
- **Review Agent** (lines 65-79): Very short, needs constraints and output section
- **QA Agent** (lines 81-94): Very short, needs constraints and output section
notes: Plan updated: Added beads-agent-type--expand-template helper for readable placeholders like <issue-id>. All agent prompts will use this instead of format.
title: Review and improve prompts for Task, Review, QA agent types

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-agent.el:849-854, 871-876` - `beads-agent-stop` and `beads-agent-stop-async`

## Problem
Buffer-local session ID is cleared BEFORE the backend stop is called:

```elisp
;; Clear buffer-local session ID BEFORE killing buffer
(setq-local beads-sesman--buffer-session-id nil)
(beads-agent-backend-stop backend session)  ;; May kill buffer synchronously
```

If the process sentinel fires immediately (synchronous process), the kill-buffer-hook could fire before the state-change-hook completes, causing double-cleanup attempts.

## Impact
Potential double-cleanup, race condition between hooks.

## Fix
Clear session ID AFTER backend stop, or use a guard flag to prevent re-entrancy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No double-cleanup possible
- [ ] Proper ordering of cleanup operations
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix race condition in session ID clearing

description:
## Problem

Current `beads-agent-list` is flat. Steve Yegge describes managing 30+ agents across worktrees, needing hierarchical organization.

## Solution

Enhance existing `beads-agent-list` (`lisp/beads-agent-list.el`) with grouping:

```
Agent Sessions (6 total, 4 running)

Project                    Agent      Status   Issue    Title                        Duration
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
beads.el [main]
                           Task#1     running  bd-123   Implement feature X          2h 15m
                           Review#1   idle     bd-124   Review authentication PR       45m
                           Plan#1     running  bd-130   Design caching layer         1h 02m

beads.el-bd-125 [bd-125]
                           Task#1     running  bd-125   Fix database connection bug    38m

other-project [feature-branch]
                           Task#1     idle     other-42 Add unit tests                 12m
```

## Implementation

1. Add grouping logic to `beads-agent-list--populate-buffer`
2. Sort sessions by `project-dir` before building entries
3. Insert group header rows (non-selectable) between project groups
4. Show git branch in group header via `beads--get-git-branch`
5. Add summary line in header (X total, Y running)
6. Consider adding "Project" column or using indent for visual grouping

## Current Code (`lisp/beads-agent-list.el`)

- Uses `tabulated-list-mode` with columns: Issue, Title, Backend, Status, Duration, Directory
- `beads-agent-list--session-to-entry` converts session to row
- `beads-agent-list--populate-buffer` builds the list

## Changes Needed

- Group sessions by project before rendering
- Add group header rows (special entries with project/branch info)
- Possibly add "spawn agent in this project" action on group headers

## Files to Modify

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sessions grouped by project/worktree
- [ ] Group headers show project name and git branch
- [ ] Summary shows total/running agent counts
- [ ] Non-selectable group header rows
- [ ] Sorting works within groups
- [ ] Existing keybindings still work

description:
Add an agent type layer to beads.el that defines different kinds of AI work (Task, Review, Plan, QA, Custom). Each type has a prompt, single-letter abbreviation, and customizable behavior via EIEIO. Types are orthogonal to backends - a session combines an agent type with a backend.

## Architecture
Session = Agent Type (Task/Review/Plan) + Backend (claude-code/claude-code-ide)

## Agent Types
- Task (T): Autonomous task completion using beads task-agent.md
- Review (R): Code review with customizable prompt
- Plan (P): Planning agent requiring backend plan mode
- QA (Q): Testing/quality assurance agent
- Custom (C): User-provided prompt at runtime

## Key Features
- Smart start: T jumps to existing or starts new
- C-u prefix forces new agent
- Per-type preferred backends
- Single-letter display in list view (colored by state)
- Works in both beads-list-mode and beads-show-mode
notes:
## Comprehensive Acceptance Criteria

### Epic-Level Completion
- [ ] All sub-issues closed and verified

description:
## Location
`lisp/beads-list.el:304` - `beads-list--format-agent`

## Problem
`(substring type-name 0 1)` is called without nil check on `type-name`. When `beads-agent-session-type-name` returns nil, this crashes.

## Code Path
```elisp
(let* ((type-name (and (fboundp 'beads-agent-session-type-name)
                       (beads-agent-session-type-name session)))
       ;; ...
       (substring type-name 0 1))  ;; CRASH if type-name is nil!
```

## Impact
Crash when formatting agent column in beads-list if session has no type-name set.

## Fix
Add nil check: `(when type-name (substring type-name 0 1))`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No crash when type-name is nil
- [ ] Graceful fallback display (e.g., "?" or empty)
- [ ] Excellent test coverage
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix nil type-name substring crash

description:
Add commands to switch issue focus in a directory-bound agent session.

## Purpose
Since agents now persist across issues, users need commands to:
- Switch focus to a different issue
- Clear focus (general project context)
- View touched issues

## File to Modify
`lisp/beads-agent.el`

## New Commands

### beads-agent-focus-issue
```elisp
(defun beads-agent-focus-issue (issue-id &optional session)
  "Set current agent focus to ISSUE-ID.
Adds ISSUE-ID to touched-issues if not already present.
SESSION defaults to current session."
  (interactive (list (beads-completion-read-issue "Focus on issue: ")))
  ...)
```

### beads-agent-clear-focus
```elisp
(defun beads-agent-clear-focus (&optional session)
  "Clear current issue focus, agent continues in project context.
SESSION defaults to current session."
  (interactive)
  ...)
```

### beads-agent-show-touched
```elisp
(defun beads-agent-show-touched (&optional session)
  "Show all issues touched by SESSION.
SESSION defaults to current session."
  (interactive)
  ...)
```

## Transient Integration
Add to agent transient menu:
- `f` - Focus on issue
- `F` - Clear focus
- `t` - Show touched issues

## Acceptance Criteria

- Target >90% coverage for new code in beads-agent.el and beads-agent-backend.el

### 2. Server-Client Integration Testing
**Critical**: Before closing this epic, the feature MUST be manually tested with a fresh Emacs server/client setup.

---

## Shared Integration Test Setup

All sub-issues reference this common setup procedure:

### 1. Create Temporary Test Project
```bash
export TEST_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d)/test-beads-project
mkdir -p "$TEST_PROJECT"
cd "$TEST_PROJECT"
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m "Initial commit"
bd init
bd create "Test issue A" -t task -p 2
bd create "Test issue B" -t task -p 2
```

### 2. Start Fresh Emacs Server
```bash
SERVER_NAME=beads-test  # Customize per issue if needed
emacsclient -s $SERVER_NAME -e "(kill-emacs)" 2>/dev/null || true
emacs --daemon=$SERVER_NAME
emacsclient -s $SERVER_NAME -c
```

### 3. Initialize in Emacs
```elisp
(cd "$TEST_PROJECT")  ; or navigate via find-file
(require (quote beads))
(beads-list)
```

### 4. Cleanup
```bash
emacsclient -s $SERVER_NAME -e "(kill-emacs)"
rm -rf "$TEST_PROJECT"
```

---

```
om82 ──┤                                           ├──→ hil7 (tests)
```

notes:
## Fix Applied

The bug was caused by claudemacs.el being byte-compiled without eat.el loaded. This means the `gv-setter` for `eat-term-parameter` wasn't available at compile time, so the `setf` expansion fell back to trying to call a non-existent function `(setf eat-term-parameter)`.

### Solution

Added a workaround in beads-agent-claudemacs.el that:
1. Ensures eat is loaded BEFORE claudemacs (so gv-setter definitions are available)
2. Calls `beads-agent-claudemacs--ensure-eat-gv-setter` before loading claudemacs
3. Provides advice on `claudemacs-setup-bell-handler` that catches the specific error
4. Falls back to using `eat-term-set-parameter` directly (the underlying setter function)

### Files Changed
- lisp/beads-agent-claudemacs.el - Added gv-setter fix
- lisp/test/beads-agent-claudemacs-test.el - Added 8 new tests

### Test Coverage
- All 1496 tests pass (including 8 new tests for this fix)
title: Agent start from list fails: (void-function (setf eat-term-parameter))

description:

```elisp
INITIAL-ISSUE sets current-issue and adds to touched-issues.
If nil, agent starts with general project context.")
```

## Start Flow Changes

```elisp
;; New call - project-only context
```

- [ ] Session ID uses project, not issue
- [ ] current-issue set from initial-issue
- [ ] touched-issues initialized correctly

close_reason: Fixed: removed compilation mode from terminal backend choices
description:
Compilation mode is currently the fallback terminal backend for running bd commands interactively, but it doesn't handle interactive input (prompts, confirmations). Commands like `bd doctor --fix` may ask for confirmation, which fails in compilation mode.

We should either:
1. Remove compilation mode from the backend list entirely
2. Make term-mode the default fallback instead
3. Detect when a command might need input and refuse to run in compilation mode

The current backend priority order is: vterm > eat > term > compile. Consider removing compile or making it opt-in only for known non-interactive commands.
title: Remove compilation mode as default terminal backend

description:
## Locations
- `lisp/beads-agent.el:183-195` - `beads-agent--git-command-async`
- `lisp/beads-agent.el:310-328` - `beads-agent--create-worktree-async`
- `lisp/beads-agent.el:379-398` - `beads-agent--import-worktree-issues-async`
- `lisp/beads-agent.el:405-423` - `beads-agent--create-worktree-existing-branch-async`

## Problem
If `set-process-sentinel` fires with an exception before reaching `kill-buffer`, the buffer leaks:

```elisp
(output-buffer (generate-new-buffer " *beads-git-async*"))
(process (apply #'start-process ...))
(set-process-sentinel
 process
 (lambda (proc _event)
   ;; If exception thrown here before line 194, buffer stays alive forever!
   (kill-buffer output-buffer)))
```

## Impact
Memory leak - temporary buffers accumulate on errors.

## Fix
Wrap sentinel body in `unwind-protect`:
```elisp
(set-process-sentinel
 process
 (lambda (proc _event)
   (unwind-protect
       (progn ...)  ;; Main logic
     (when (buffer-live-p output-buffer)
       (kill-buffer output-buffer)))))
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Buffers always cleaned up, even on error
- [ ] unwind-protect in all async sentinels
- [ ] Excellent test coverage for error paths
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix output buffer leaks on exception in async operations

description:
## Locations
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code.el:135`
- `lisp/beads-agent-claude-code-ide.el:143`
- `lisp/beads-agent-claudemacs.el:255`

## Problem
Backends call `(car (find-buffers ...))` immediately after starting the external process. There's a race condition where:
1. Backend process is spawned
2. Code immediately searches for the buffer (which may not yet exist)
3. May return `nil` if the buffer hasn't been created yet

```elisp
(claude-code)  ;; Returns immediately, spawns process
(setq buffer (car (beads-agent-claude-code--find-buffers working-dir)))  ;; May be nil!
```

## Impact
Sessions may start with `buffer = nil`, breaking buffer lifecycle and session UI integration. The `beads-agent-backend-start` protocol expects `(backend-session . buffer)` but buffer may be nil.

## Fix Options
1. Add retry logic with timeout for buffer acquisition
2. Use callback-based buffer acquisition
3. Have upstream tools return buffer directly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Buffer is never nil when returned from start
- [ ] Graceful handling if buffer creation times out
- [ ] Excellent test coverage for timing edge cases
- [ ] Tested in live Emacs session inside tmux
title: Fix buffer acquisition race condition in terminal backends

This should be easily accessible (similar to Magit's push/pull), as
syncing is a frequent operation in collaborative workflows.

description:
## Problem

When beads starts a claudemacs session, it immediately renames the buffer from `*claudemacs:TOOL:SESSION-ID*` to `*beads-agent[ISSUE-ID][TYPE#N]*`.

However, claudemacs uses deferred timers (up to 10 retries × 0.5s) to set up eat integration. When these timers fire, `claudemacs-setup-bell-handler` calls `claudemacs--get-current-session-buffer` which looks for buffers matching the `*claudemacs:...*` pattern - but the buffer has been renamed.

## Current Workaround

We catch `wrong-type-argument` errors in our advice and fall back to a fixed implementation that finds buffers by mode instead of name pattern.

## Better Solutions

1. **Don't rename claudemacs buffers** - Keep original name, just store buffer reference in session. The session already tracks the buffer object.

2. **Delay rename for claudemacs** - Wait ~6 seconds for setup timers to complete before renaming. Fragile but would work.

3. **Backend-specific rename control** - Add a method to backend protocol to control whether/how buffers are renamed.

4. **File upstream PR to claudemacs** - Add buffer name customization option, or make `claudemacs--get-current-session-buffer` find by mode instead of name pattern.

## Related

- beads.el-qp2e: Claudemacs startup error: wrong-type-argument stringp nil
title: Avoid renaming claudemacs buffers or delay until setup completes

description:
When using vterm as the terminal backend for interactive command execution, the buffer disappears after the command finishes. Sometimes it disappears immediately, making it impossible to see the output.

This is likely related to vterm-kill-buffer-on-exit being set or the process sentinel killing the buffer prematurely.

Expected behavior: The buffer should remain visible so users can review command output, similar to how compilation-mode keeps its buffer.

Possibly related to the unused lexical variable warning in beads-command.el:185:
  'Unused lexical variable vterm-kill-buffer-on-exit'
title: vterm buffer disappears after command completes

description:
Currently beads-create.el has its own validation functions that duplicate
validation logic that should live in the beads-command-create class. This
creates maintenance burden and inconsistency.

## Current State

beads-create.el has duplicate validation:
- beads-create--validate-title
- beads-create--validate-type  
- beads-create--validate-priority
- beads-create--validate-dependencies
- beads-create--validate-all (calls all the above)

beads-command.el already has:
- beads-command-validate method for beads-command-create
- Validates basic constraints (title or file, list types)

## Problem

1. Validation logic is duplicated across two files
2. beads-create.el validates BEFORE calling beads-command-execute
3. beads-command-execute validates AGAIN internally
4. No single source of truth for validation rules
5. beads-update.el already uses the better pattern (calls beads-command-validate)

## Better Pattern (from beads-update.el)

beads-update.el:170 shows the right approach:
```elisp
(defun beads-update--validate-all (cmd)
  "Validate all parameters from CMD command instance."
  (let ((error-msg (beads-command-validate cmd))
        (changes (beads-update--get-changed-fields cmd)))
    (delq nil
          (list
           error-msg
           (when (null changes) "No fields have been changed")))))
```

It:
- Calls beads-command-validate (reuses command class validation)
- Adds only UI-specific validation (e.g., "no fields changed")
title: Refactor beads-create.el validation to use beads-command-validate method

description:
Add rich completing-read experience for AI agent backend selection with annotations and marginalia support.

## Current State
The current `beads-reader-agent-backend` uses simple `completing-read` with plain backend names - no annotations or grouping.

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Add Helper (beads-agent-backend.el)
Add `beads-agent--get-all-backends` to return all backends (not just available):
```elisp
(defun beads-agent--get-all-backends ()
  "Return list of all registered backends, sorted by priority."
  beads-agent--backends)
```

### Step 2: Add Completion Functions (beads-completion.el)

**Completion table:**
```elisp
(defun beads-completion-backend-table ()
  "Return completion table for AI agent backends with annotations."
  (lambda (string pred action)
    (if (eq action 'metadata)
        '(metadata
          (category . beads-agent-backend)
          (annotation-function . beads-completion--backend-annotate)
          (group-function . beads-completion--backend-group))
      (let ((backends (beads-agent--get-all-backends)))
        (complete-with-action
         action
         (mapcar (lambda (b)
                   (propertize (oref b name)
                               'beads-backend b
                               'beads-available (beads-agent-backend-available-p b)))
                 backends)
         string pred)))))
```

**Annotation function:** Shows [P#] and availability status with faces
**Group function:** Groups by Available/Unavailable

### Step 3: Update Reader (beads-reader.el)
Replace `beads-reader-agent-backend` to use new completion table with validation.

### Step 4: Add Tests
Add tests to test/beads-completion-test.el

## Expected Result
```
Backend: claudemacs    [P10] Available
         agent-shell   [P20] Available
         dummy-backend [P99] Unavailable
```

With Vertico grouping:
```
-- Available --
claudemacs    [P10] Available
agent-shell   [P20] Available
-- Unavailable --
dummy-backend [P99] Unavailable
```

## Marginalia Integration
No special code needed - marginalia automatically uses the `beads-agent-backend` completion category and our `annotation-function` from metadata.

## Files to Modify
1. lisp/beads-agent-backend.el - Add helper function
2. lisp/beads-completion.el - Add completion table and annotation functions
3. lisp/beads-reader.el - Update reader to use rich completion
4. test/beads-completion-test.el - Add tests
title: Add rich completion with marginalia support for AI agent backend selection

description:
Implement Emacs interface for the 'bd daemon' command.

The bd daemon command runs a background sync daemon that automatically synchronizes issues with git remote at configurable intervals.

**Features:**
- Start daemon with auto-commit/push capabilities
- Stop running daemon
- Check daemon status

**Implementation:**

  - --auto-commit: Automatically commit changes
  - --auto-push: Automatically push commits
  - --interval: Sync check interval (duration)
  - --log: Log file path
  - --status: Show daemon status
  - --stop: Stop running daemon
- Display daemon status in modeline or dedicated buffer
- Show log output in buffer
- Start/stop controls

description:
attached_args: Finish migrating remaining 8 command classes to EIEIO slot metadata. Use the shiny workflow: design, implement, review, test, submit.
dispatched_by: mayor

After successful proof of concept with beads-command-create, migrate all remaining
command classes to use slot metadata.

## Commands to Migrate

1. **beads-command-update** - Update issues
2. **beads-command-close** - Close issues
3. **beads-command-reopen** - Reopen issues
4. **beads-command-show** - Show issue details
5. **beads-command-list** - List issues (complex, many filter slots)
6. **beads-command-ready** - Show ready issues
7. **beads-command-blocked** - Show blocked issues
8. **beads-command-init** - Initialize beads
9. **beads-command-export** - Export issues
10. **beads-command-import** - Import issues
11. **beads-command-stats** - Show statistics
12. **beads-command-dep-add** - Add dependency
13. **beads-command-dep-remove** - Remove dependency
14. **beads-command-dep-tree** - Show dependency tree
15. **beads-command-dep-cycles** - Detect cycles
16. **beads-command-label-add** - Add label
17. **beads-command-label-remove** - Remove label
18. **beads-command-epic-close-eligible** - Close eligible epics
19. **beads-command-epic-status** - Epic status

## Migration Process Per Command

1. Add slot properties to class definition
2. Test command-line generation matches original
3. Remove manual beads-command-line method
4. Update/remove corresponding transient code
5. Run tests for that module

## Priority Order

1. High-use commands: update, close, list, show
2. Medium-use: dep-*, label-*, ready, blocked
3. Lower-use: init, export, import, epic-*, stats

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 19+ command classes migrated
- [ ] All manual beads-command-line methods removed
- [ ] All manual parse functions removed
- [ ] Significant code reduction achieved
- [ ] All tests pass

description:
Create a dedicated `*beads-agents*` buffer using `tabulated-list-mode` to display all active AI agent sessions with useful keybindings.

## Entry Point
- `beads-agent-list` - Main command to open the agent list buffer

## Current State (to be replaced)
- `beads-agent-list-sessions` uses simple `with-help-window` text display
- No tabulated list mode, no keybindings, no sorting
- **This function should be removed** and replaced with proper tabulated list

## Deliverables

### New File: `lisp/beads-agent-list.el`

**Buffer**: `*beads-agents*`

**Columns** (with customizable widths via defcustom):
| Column | Width | Content | Notes |
|--------|-------|---------|-------|
| Issue | 12 | Issue ID | Primary identifier |
| Title | 30 | Issue title | Batch-fetched, cached in buffer |
| Backend | 15 | Backend name | claude-code-ide, efrit |
| Status | 10 | running/stale/done/failed | Propertized with face |
| Duration | 10 | "2h 15m" | Time since started |
| Directory | 25 | Working dir | Worktree or project |

**Faces** (inherit from existing patterns):
- `beads-agent-list-running` - inherit success, bold
- `beads-agent-list-stale` - inherit shadow
- `beads-agent-list-finished` - inherit success
- `beads-agent-list-failed` - inherit error

**Keybindings**:
| Key | Command | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| RET | `beads-agent-list-jump` | Jump to agent buffer |
| j | `beads-agent-list-jump` | Jump to agent buffer |
| i | `beads-agent-list-show-issue` | Show issue details |
| s | `beads-agent-list-stop` | Stop session at point |
| S | `beads-agent-list-stop-all` | Stop all sessions |
| r | `beads-agent-list-restart` | Restart session |
| g | `beads-agent-list-refresh` | Refresh |
| q | `quit-window` | Quit |
| c | `beads-agent-list-cleanup` | Cleanup stale |
| n/p | Navigation | Next/previous line |
| w | `beads-agent-list-copy-session-id` | Copy session ID |
| W | `beads-agent-list-copy-issue-id` | Copy issue ID |
| C-c C-s | `beads-sesman-map` | Sesman commands |

### Integration Points
- Update `beads-agent--list-suffix` to call `beads-agent-list`
- Remove deprecated `beads-agent-list-sessions` function
- Hook into `beads-agent-state-change-hook` for auto-refresh

## Data Sources
Use existing functions from `beads-agent-backend.el`:
- `beads-agent--get-all-sessions` - get all sessions
- `beads-agent--session-active-p` - check if active
- `beads-agent--get-backend` - get backend instance
- `beads-agent--get-issue-outcome` - get finished/failed status

## Pattern Reference
Follow `beads-list.el` implementation patterns:
- `tabulated-list-mode` derived mode
- Customizable column widths via defcustom
- Faces for visual distinction
- Standard refresh mechanism
- Hook-based auto-refresh (no polling)

## Files to Create/Modify
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `lisp/beads-agent-list.el` | CREATE - Main implementation |
| `lisp/test/beads-agent-list-test.el` | CREATE - Tests |
| `lisp/beads-agent.el` | MODIFY - Update suffix, remove old function |

## Design Decisions
1. **Issue title column**: YES - batch-fetch titles, cache in buffer-local hash
2. **Marking support**: NO for v1 - keep simple
3. **Auto-refresh**: Hook-based only (on state changes)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Compile clean, lint clean, unit tests pass

### Live Integration Test
**Required**: Live test of functionality in an Emacs server controlled by emacsclient, simulating actual user interaction:
- [ ] Start Emacs server with beads.el loaded
- [ ] Use emacsclient to invoke `beads-agent-list`
- [ ] Verify buffer opens with correct columns and format
- [ ] Test keybindings (navigation, jump, stop, refresh, copy)
- [ ] Test auto-refresh when agent state changes
- [ ] Test with zero sessions (empty state)
- [ ] Test with multiple sessions from different backends

### Functional Checklist
- [ ] `M-x beads-agent-list` opens tabulated buffer
- [ ] All keybindings functional
- [ ] Buffer auto-refreshes on agent state changes
- [ ] `beads-agent-list-sessions` removed from codebase
title: Agent list buffer with beads-agent-list entry point

notes:
When an agent buffer is killed manually (e.g., via `kill-buffer` or `C-x k`), the beads-list view still shows the agent as running.

## Expected behavior
- Agent indicator in list view should reflect actual agent state
- When buffer is killed, agent should be marked as stopped/removed

## Actual behavior
- Agent still appears active in beads-list after buffer is killed
- User sees stale status

## Root cause (likely)
The agent session registry (`beads-agent--sessions`) is not updated when the buffer is killed externally. Need to either:
1. Add a `kill-buffer-hook` to detect buffer death and clean up session
2. Make `beads-agent-session-active-p` check if buffer still exists
3. Both: hook for cleanup + active check as fallback

## Affected components
- beads-agent.el (session management)
- beads-list.el (agent column display)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated to cover the fix
- [ ] Fix tested in live Emacs session and confirmed working
- [ ] All existing tests pass
title: Agent list view not updated when agent buffer killed manually

description:
When pressing P in the list view when an agent is already started:

1. Shows misleading message: "Claude Code session expired, starting new one... [2 times]"
2. Hides the agent buffer if it is already visible

## Expected Behavior

- If the agent session is still active, should just jump to the visible buffer
- Should not show "session expired" message for active sessions
- Should not hide the buffer if it's already visible - just switch to it

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start an agent on an issue (e.g., with P from list view)
2. Agent buffer becomes visible
3. Switch to list view
4. Press P on the same issue again
5. See misleading "expired" message and buffer behavior

## Possible Fix

Check if session is active before attempting to start a new one. If active:
- Just call `beads-agent-jump` or switch to the existing buffer
- Don't show session expired message
title: Agent P command shows expired session message and hides visible buffer

close_reason: Already fixed - gastown Go code correctly handles bd show --json array response. Both sling.go:getBeadInfo() and beads.go:Show() unmarshal into []Issue and take the first element. Verified with gt sling --dry-run.
description:
## Problem

gt sling command fails with 'no issue found' error when trying to sling valid issues.

## Root Cause

bd show <id> --json returns an array [{...}] instead of an object {...}.

gt sling validation code expects object format and uses jq -r '.id' which fails on arrays.

## Evidence

bd show bde-koh7 --json returns: [{"id":"bde-koh7",...}]
Should return: {"id":"bde-koh7",...}

## Fix Needed

This is a gastown CLI (gt) bug, not a beads bug. The fix should be in the gastown source code where gt sling validates beads.

Update gt sling bead validation to handle array responses:
- Change from: jq -r '.id'
- Change to: jq -r '.[0].id // .id' (handles both formats)

## Impact

All gt sling operations fail, blocking work assignment to polecats.

## Workaround

Use gt mail send or bd slot set to assign work instead of slinging.
title: gt sling fails: bd show --json returns array instead of object

description:
Implement automatic transient prefix (menu) generation from command class slot metadata.

## Implementation

Create functions/macros to auto-generate complete transient menus:

1. `beads-meta-generate-groups` - Generate transient groups from slot metadata
2. `beads-meta-generate-prefix` - Generate complete transient prefix
3. `beads-meta-define-prefix` - Macro to define prefix at compile time

## How It Works

1. Collect all slots with `:transient-key` property
2. Group slots by `:transient-group` property
3. Order groups by minimum `:transient-level` in group
4. Order slots within groups by `:transient-order`
5. Generate transient-define-group for each group
6. Generate transient-define-prefix combining all groups

## Group Generation

```elisp
;; From slots with :transient-group "Required"
;; Generates:
(transient-define-group beads-create--required-section
  [:level 1 "Required"
   (beads-create-infix-title)])

;; From slots with :transient-group "Issue attributes"
;; Generates:
(transient-define-group beads-create--issue-attributes-section
  [:level 2 "Issue attributes"
   (beads-create-infix-type)
   (beads-create-infix-priority)
   (beads-create-infix-assignee)
   (beads-create-infix-labels)])
```

## Prefix Generation

```elisp
;; Generates complete prefix:
(transient-define-prefix beads-create ()
  "Create a new issue."
  beads-create--required-section
  beads-create--issue-attributes-section
  beads-create--content-section
  beads-create--advanced-section
  beads-option-global-section
  ["Actions"
   (beads-create--execute)
   (beads-create--preview)
   (beads-create--reset)])
```

## Standard Suffixes

Every generated prefix includes standard action suffixes:
- Execute (x) - Run the command
- Preview (P) - Show command that will run
- Reset (R) - Clear all fields

These can be customized via class properties or overridden.

## Parse Function Generation

Also generate `beads-<cmd>--parse-transient-args` function that:
1. Takes transient args list
2. Maps each arg back to corresponding slot
3. Creates command instance with populated slots

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `beads-meta-generate-groups` function implemented
- [ ] `beads-meta-generate-prefix` function implemented
- [ ] Groups organized by level and order
- [ ] Standard suffixes included
- [ ] Parse function auto-generated
- [ ] Works with existing beads-option-global-section
- [ ] ERT tests covering generation
- [ ] Lint and compile pass
title: Implement automatic transient prefix generation from slot metadata

description:
When running M-x beads-create (or accessing it from M-x beads), Emacs raises an error: transient-setup: Wrong type argument: number-or-marker-p, #s(transient-prefix ...).

Root cause: Line 266 of beads-create.el contains 'beads-create-infix-arguments (quoted symbol) which should be beads-create-infix-arguments (unquoted). The quote causes transient to receive a symbol instead of evaluating it properly.

This prevents users from using the beads-create transient menu.
notes:
Complete fix applied:
1. Restored transient-define-group for beads-create-infix-arguments (following Magit pattern)
2. Reference the group UNQUOTED in transient-define-prefix (as first element after docstring)
3. Removed problematic :if lambda checking transient-current-prefix - transient handles level visibility automatically via (7 ...) prefixes
4. Added pad-keys slot to beads-option-global class - required when using custom infix classes with transient-columns
5. All tests pass, compile succeeds
title: Bug: Quoted symbol in beads-create causes transient-setup error
